title: Controls Engineer
description: **Field Service Engineer, Controls** Location: Dublin and with travel across Ireland with some international trips. We are looking for an experienced Field Service Engineer to commission, optimise and support automation and control systems on bespoke production equipment. This is a predominantly site\-based, hands\-on role working directly on customer machinery and control systems, often with minimal supervision. You will ensure equipment is installed, started up and running reliably, while maintaining high technical standards and a professional, customer\-focused approach. **Key Responsibilities** * Configure, modify and support PLC, HMI and related PC\-based control software in line with customer process and safety requirements * Commission, fault\-find and optimise automation and control systems at client sites to achieve agreed performance and uptime targets * Diagnose electrical, pneumatic and control issues using schematics, manuals and diagnostic tools, implementing robust and sustainable fixes * Plan and execute site work independently or as part of a wider project team, coordinating with customer production schedules * Align on\-site activities with customer objectives, ensuring work is clearly planned, communicated and documented * Share operational and troubleshooting knowledge with maintenance and operations teams, providing training and guidance where needed * Recommend tools, spare parts or equipment improvements to enhance reliability, maintainability and performance * Make balanced recommendations on repair versus replacement, focusing on cost\-effective, long\-term solutions * Lead and coordinate site activities involving internal colleagues, customer personnel and third\-party vendors * Produce clear visit reports and service documentation, tracking open actions through to resolution * Participate in an on\-call rota to support customers outside standard hours when required * Communicate effectively with internal stakeholders and customer contacts at all levels * Support warranty and non\-warranty service activities in line with company processes * Work safely at all times, following site rules and company health and safety procedures * Represent the company professionally on customer sites as a trusted technical point of contact **Qualifications** * Degree or technical qualification in Electrical, Automation or Control Engineering, or a closely related discipline * Experience with databases, robotics or vision systems is advantageous but not essential **Experience and Skills** * Several years’ experience in a practical automation or control systems role, typically 4\+ years * Strong background developing, modifying and troubleshooting PLC and HMI applications, ideally on Allen\-Bradley and or Siemens platforms * Solid understanding of electrical, pneumatic and multi\-axis control systems * Confident reading and interpreting technical drawings, wiring diagrams and system documentation * Good working knowledge of Microsoft Word and Excel for reporting and documentation * Demonstrated ability to learn new systems quickly and troubleshoot complex automated equipment * Willingness and flexibility to travel extensively, sometimes at short notice * Experience within life sciences or pharmaceutical manufacturing is beneficial but not required **About You** You are a clear and confident communicator who builds effective working relationships with engineers, operators, vendors and management. You stay calm under pressure, organise your workload effectively and take ownership of issues through to resolution. You enjoy being on site, solving real\-world technical problems and delivering a high standard of service without close supervision.

title: Data Analyst

We’re a family\-founded company on a mission to create the world’s first AI\-powered Personal \& Entrepreneurial Resource Planner (PRP), and we need your passion and ambition to help us change how people plan, work, and live. Born in Lisbon, Portugal in 2018—and now with offices in both Lisbon and San Francisco—we’ve remained self\-funded while reaching over 100 million downloads worldwide. Our long\-term focus drives us to challenge the status quo every day, pushing the boundaries of AI\-driven solutions that truly make a difference. Here, you’ll be a creative force, shaping products that empower people across the globe. Join us on this journey to redefine resource management—and change lives along the way. ****The Role**** As a **Data Analyst** at Air Apps, you will be responsible for **analyzing and interpreting data** to drive business insights and optimize product performance. You will create **reports, dashboards, and performance metrics** to help teams make informed decisions and improve user experience. This role requires a **data\-driven mindset, strong analytical skills, and experience with visualization tools** to turn complex data into actionable insights. * This is a fully onsite position, based at our office in Lisbon, where you will collaborate closely with cross\-functional teams in person and contribute to a dynamic and fast\-paced environment. We are open to support with relocation efforts. **Responsibilities** * Analyze user behavior, product performance, and key business metrics to identify trends and opportunities. * Create and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using tools like Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or Google Data Studio. * Collect, clean, and structure large datasets to ensure accuracy and usability. * Define and track KPIs for marketing, product, and business performance. * Work with SQL, Python, or R to extract, analyze, and manipulate data. * Collaborate with product managers, engineers, and marketing teams to provide data\-driven insights. * Conduct A/B testing and experiment analysis to improve product features and user engagement. * Identify bottlenecks and opportunities in user journeys and customer funnels. * Present findings in a clear and compelling way to stakeholders and leadership teams. * Stay up to date with industry trends, analytics best practices, and emerging data tools. **Requirements** * Around 3\+ years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or a related field. * Proficiency in SQL for data querying and manipulation. * Experience with data visualization tools (Tableau, Looker, Power BI, Google Data Studio). * Strong analytical skills with experience in Python or R for data analysis. * Knowledge of A/B testing methodologies and statistical analysis. * Experience with Google Analytics, Mixpanel, Amplitude, or similar user analytics tools. * Strong problem\-solving skills and ability to communicate insights to non\-technical stakeholders. * Experience working with large datasets and cloud\-based data warehouses (BigQuery, Redshift, Snowflake). * Familiarity with ETL processes and data pipeline optimization is a plus. * Ability to work in a fast\-paced, data\-driven environment and manage multiple projects. ****What benefits are we offering?**** * Apple hardware ecosystem for work. * Annual Bonus * Top\-tier Health and Life Insurance for peace of mind. * Transportation Budget to support your commute needs. * Coverflex benefits package for meal allowances, well\-being, and more. * Childcare support. * Air Conference \- an opportunity to meet the team, collaborate, and grow together. * Pension Fund to support your long\-term financial planning. * Urban Sports Club membership to keep you active. * Meals 100% free at the hub. ****Diversity \& Inclusion**** At Air Apps, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace. We enthusiastically welcome applicants from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We celebrate diversity in all its forms and believe that varied voices and experiences make us stronger. ****Application Disclaimer**** At Air Apps, we value transparency and integrity in our hiring process. Applicants must submit their own work without any AI\-generated assistance. Any use of AI in application materials, assessments, or interviews will result in disqualification. Compensation Range: €50K \- €63K

title: Data Analyst
description: **Data Analyst – Digital Fraud \& Security** Dublin (Hybrid) 12\-Month Contract initially Competitive Daily Rate **The Opportunity** We are looking for a sharp, technically\-oriented **Data Analyst** to join a premier Fraud Detection and Security (FDS) team within the financial services sector. This role is a fantastic opportunity for an analyst who wants to specialise in the detection and prevention of digital fraud. The position is designed for professional growth. You will initially focus on daily quality assurance (QA), metrics, and reporting. As you master the team’s mission and technical landscape, you will transition into a core investigative role, performing deep\-dive fraud event analysis and identifying emerging threats. **Key Responsibilities** * **Data Investigation:** Analyse large datasets to identify abnormal behaviour, system abuse, and signatures of malware or fraudster techniques. * **QA \& Metrics:** Lead the daily quality assurance of fraud alerts to ensure consistency with procedures, creating daily, weekly, and monthly reports for senior stakeholders. * **Strategic Input:** Recommend application\-level solutions to prevent abuse and assist in implementing controls regarding new bank policies. * **Technical Research:** Leverage your understanding of technical architecture and application design to research complex alerts and communicate the impact of identified issues. **Required Skills:** * **Technical Analysis:** Strong proficiency in data analytics and data aggregation tools with the ability to extract and interpret large, complex datasets. * **Security Mindset:** Knowledgeable in common application security vulnerabilities and a keen interest in staying up\-to\-date with malware trends. * **Process Management:** Exceptional organisational skills with the ability to work independently in a fast\-paced environment. * **Communication:** Excellent verbal and written skills, with the ability to translate technical findings into clear business insights. **Highly Desirable:** * **Splunk:** Previous experience with Splunk (query language, data ingestion, and analysis) is a significant advantage. * **Development:** Familiarity with web application development or common scripting languages. * **Security Tooling:** Experience with SIEM, EDR, or Big Data platforms used for actioning threat intelligence.

title: Demand Planner
description: **About Us** Home to the iconic Instantor® Brand, Sanbra Fyffe is a market leader in the manufacturing, wholesale and distribution of plumbing supplies to the builder’s merchants trade for 90 years. At Instantor, our values aren’t just words on a page \- they shape how we work, support each other, and deliver for our customers. We’re looking for someone who shares these values and wants to thrive in a collaborative, customer\-focused environment. **Our Values** Teamwork: All departments within the business act as one team to make Sanbra Fyffe the best it can be. Customer Focus: We commit to meet and exceed our customer’s expectations, by placing the customer at the centre of all our decisions. Quality \& Reliability: We focus on doing things the right way, the first time; delivering on quality, on time and on cost. Expertise: We develop and manufacture solutions to provide our customers with maximum value. **Role Purpose** Due to strong growth within the business, we are expanding our Purchasing Team and looking to hire an experienced Demand Planner. This is an excellent opportunity and a fantastic time for an ambitious individual to join a high\-paced, growing Purchasing Department at the company’s HQ in Santry, Dublin. This role will play a critical role in improving demand planning and forecasting practices, acting as a key link between commercial and operational functions. The Demand Planner will own the integrity and output of newly implemented demand planning tool, to enable the purchasing team to make confident, timely decisions and improve overall stock availability. **Key Responsibilities** * Maintain and uphold the integrity of the third\-party demand planning tool and act as key user, ensuring that the output of the tool is trustworthy and actionable for the purchasers * Participate in forecast review and validation process, provide clear reporting on forecast accuracy and act as a key player in strengthening S\&OP practices into the business * Refine sales forecasts for existing products, collecting, checking and analysing all necessary information to increase overall forecasting accuracy and service level * Lead out\-of\-stock reporting and root cause analysis to minimize lost sales * Pro\-actively identify future inventory risks and opportunities and liaise with Purchasing, Operations and Sales teams with actionable recommendations * Manage phase\-in and phase\-out implications on forecast of obsolescent ranges and new product introductions, translating these insights into the demand planning tool * Acts as a forecasting interface between purchasing and sales departments, translating commercial insights into forecast adjustments in the demand planning tool * Work closely with Operations and Sales teams to refine inventory level targets in 3PL * Work within an experienced and expanding Purchasing department **Key Requirements** * Relevant third level qualification; title: Disputes & Investigations Lawyer
description: We currently have opportunities for bright, personable and enthusiastic lawyers to join our dynamic team within our market leading firm. We would like to hear from any interested solicitors, whether practicing locally or considering returning to Ireland, with 3\-6 years relevant PQE gained within a large, medium or boutique corporate law firm. Our Disputes \& Investigations group is the leading disputes and investigations team in the Irish market. We specialise in resolving complex, high value and cross border commercial disputes, through litigation, arbitration, mediation, negotiation and expert determination. We combine this contentious experience with our leading regulatory and white collar expertise to advise on the most complex, challenging and high profile corporate crises for our Irish and international client base. Our clients include some of the largest Irish and global companies across a broad range of industry sectors including tech, financial services, life sciences, real estate, asset management, manufacturing, automotive, retail, agri\-food and gaming. To an ever increasing extent, our clients are required to deal with regulatory enquiries in parallel with actual or threatened civil litigation and an evolving legal landscape. We have extensive experience in designing and implementing legal strategies to respond to these types of complex mandates for our clients \- including leading engagements with a range of regulators, overseeing internal investigations, providing strategic board advice, managing stakeholder relationships, protecting the company's reputation and assisting with any employment fallout, alongside managing litigation risk. Our team has particular focus on contentious mandates where there is a consumer or customer angle, including product liability litigation and multi\-party actions. **Requirements** Successful applicants should have experience or interest in some or all of the following areas: * Advising on disputes and contentious issues for technology companies * Advising on business critical commercial disputes and adopting innovative legal strategies to dealing with claims brought by cohorts of customers * Advising on deploying various methods of dispute resolution, including litigation, mediation, expert determination and arbitration, as the most mainstream * Advising on defamation claims/cases * Advising on white collar crime issues impacting corporates (including fraud, bribery/corruption, money laundering and corporate offences) * Experience of engaging with regulators (for example the Data Protection Commission, Comisiún na Meán, the Competition and Consumer Protection Commission) in response to all forms of regulatory enquiries, including information requests, investigations, and in the context of 'dawn raids' **Benefits** **What We Offer** At A\&L we pride ourselves on the high calibre of our employees. We place strong emphasis on career development and enabling our people to excel in their careers. Our work environment is friendly, professional and collaborative. In addition to working with a market leading team we offer: * A competitive remuneration package * Access to the firm's marquee training and development programmes, delivered through the ALG School of Business and Law * Discretionary performance bonus * Pension and health insurance contribution, wellbeing contribution, life assurance, subsidised gym, restaurant and coffee shop * Hybrid working policy **About the firm:** A\&L Goodbody (ALG) is a leading Irish law firm with five international offices in Dublin, Belfast, London, New York and San Francisco. There are currently 120 partners and over 900 employees. The strong international and domestic business community in Ireland forms a valuable part of the ALG client base. ALG is incredibly proud to be recognised as the leading Irish law firm in the Chambers and Partners Europe 2024 Guide with the highest number of Band 1 practice area rankings. ALG is also ranked as a 'top tier' law firm in The Legal 500 EMEA Guide 2024 and the IFLR1000 Guide 2024\. The firm is consistently ranked number one in the Irish market and on a European level. A sample of other recent notable awards include: * Awarded the prestigious Company of the Year award at the National Diversity \& Inclusion Awards 2025 hosted by the Irish Centre for Diversity * Recognised by Mergermarket as the \#1 M\&A legal advisor in Ireland by deal volume for 2024 * Winner of the Best Client Service Award by the Lawyer European Awards * Future of Legal Services Innovation Award winners 2024 * Awarded the Investors in Diversity Silver Mark in 2024 by the Irish Centre for Diversity * Most Popular Graduate Recruiter in Law 2024 by GradIreland (for 13 consecutive years) * Law firm of the year \- Republic of Ireland 2023 at The Lawyer European Awards * Irish Law Firm of the Year 2023 at the Chambers Europe Awards * One of the top 50 most innovative law firms in Europe 2023 by the Financial Times * Best client support 2023 by the Managing Partners Forum * Business Working Responsibly Mark 2023 ALG has a reputation for leading the way in continuously developing its service to clients and internally to its business. This is delivered through the use of new processes, ways of working, innovative use of technology and marketing\-leading business service teams.

title: Electrical Apprentice - Field Service Engineer
description: At ABB, we help industries outrun \- leaner and cleaner. Here, progress is an expectation \- for you, your team, and the world. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Proficiency in Microsoft Office. * Be able to work independently and as part of a team with a logical and problem\-solving mindset. Full Clean Driving License as travel to both customers and ABB sites will be required as part of the role. More about us As part of our dedication to our employees, ABB Ireland offers an attractive array of benefits which include: * Salary * Paid overtime * Annual leave entitlement * On the job Training In an ever\-changing world, industry is facing fluctuating energy and operating costs and an insecure energy supply while demand for electrification continues to increase. At the same time, companies are under pressure to cut emissions and evolve their business models as their electrical assets get older and available investment is shrinking. We value people from diverse backgrounds. Could this be your story? title: Electrical Maintenance Technician
description: **Background:** Veolia is Ireland’s leading environmental services company. We provide a comprehensive range of energy, waste and water solutions and are dedicated to carbon reduction, protecting the environment and building the circular economy. We offer all employees a benefits package as standard which includes full VHI cover, pension and education assistance. When you see the world as we do, you see the chance to help the world take better care of its resources, and help it become a better place for everyone. It's why we're looking for someone who's just as committed as we are, to push for genuine change and bring our ambition of Ecological Transformation to life. We know that everyone here at Veolia can help us work alongside our communities, look after the environment, and contribute to our inclusive culture. **Electrical Maintenance Technician** **Location: Blanchardstown** **Hybrid/Onsite: Onsite** **Working Pattern (Time/Hours): 39 Hours per week (07:30 to 16:00\)** **Duration: Permanent** **Overview of the role:** The Utilities Maintenance Technician is responsible for the execution of the preventive and corrective maintenance program as per client requirements in a safe, efficient and competent manner in line with cGMP and site protocols and procedures. He/she will work closely with day and shift colleagues and leads to ensure all work is carried out safely, correctly and as efficiently as possible. **Duties of the Role include;** * All work on site to be carried out in accordance with site EHS (Environmental Health and Safety) and Veolia EHS and cGMP rules and guidelines; any irregularities to be reported to the Maintenance \& Calibration Lead or Contract Manager. * Carry out work as per schedule supplied by the Maintenance \& Calibrations Lead, Planner and as per site policies, schedules and procedures. * Troubleshoot problems with utilities equipment as necessary and as directed by site supervisors * Complete site documentation before and after work tasks, as appropriate * Liaise with Contractors during planned service maintenance. * Participate in the appropriate training. * Carry out Risk Assessment of Equipment Maintenance Task List steps and ensure these are always kept valid. * Carry out Periodic reviews of WI’s/SOP to ensure documents are always current and valid. * Flexibility to work on equipment in all buildings * Work and interact with the client utilities engineering team * Flexibility to work around maintenance shutdown’s * Be familiar with all GMP rules and regulations * Be familiar with statutory requirements * Be responsible for his/her training curricula and ensure missing requirements are completed * Maintenance of housekeeping standards compatible with pharmaceutical manufacturing in the general plant, control rooms and compatibility with clean room requirements as required. Responsible for applying 5 S methodology in all areas. * Implement and participate in utilities reliability programs * If/when required, provide technical input/ support during the implementation of new projects from design through to beneficial use. **Required skills for this role;** * Senior Trades, City \& Guilds or equivalent in electrical engineering. * Electrical experience, 2 years minimum and good understanding of the basic fundamentals of the various utilities systems. ***Essential:*** * Experience on the following: + Pumps, HVAC Systems, Compressors, Boilers, steam systems and ancillary services, (DHW skids), Chillers, Cooling Towers, Clean steam Boilers, Purified Water Systems, CIP systems, Low /Medium Voltage Electrical Systems. * Good understanding of Preventive Maintenance Systems. * Work within a Computerized Maintenance Management Work Order system environment. * Work within a documented Permit to Work System * Computer literate * Ability to read and understand P\&ID’s. * Understanding of SOP’s and GMP Engineering systems. * Understanding of Basic Instrumentation * Must be able to demonstrate hands on experience with detailed knowledge/skill in the following area’s: * Team player. * Attention to detail * Methodical \& analytical approach to work. * Good problem solving and trouble shooting ability. * Good Initiative and pro\-active attitude in approach to duties ***Desirable:*** * Site Level 1 Electrical Qualification * Building Management Systems (Scada) * Experience of waste water treatment plants; * PLC’s * Troubleshooting / repair of mechanical systems **Please note:** If you are an EU/EEA national, you will be asked to show proof of right to work in Ireland. If you are a non\-EU/EEA national , you will require current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. Information on legislation and guides to the procedures in relation to obtaining greencard permits ,work permits, spousal/dependant permits is available on the Department of Jobs, Enterprise and Innovation website.

title: Employee Experience Coordinator
description: **About Smartbox:** At Smartbox Group, all our energy and creativity are focused on providing the most beautiful gift to offer and to receive . **With nearly 15,000 different products** across leisure themes — Stays, Wellness, Adventure, Gastronomy — we allow people to enrich their lives with moments of sharing, emotions, and unforgettable memories. Present in **11 countries** , with **7 million experience gifts sold per year** and a business volume of nearly **€500 million** , Smartbox Group employs 700 **people across Europe** and works with over **41,000 trusted partners** (hotels, restaurants, wellness centres, outdoor activity providers…). Living more than owning, sharing more than having — this is more than ever a fundamental trend in our society, and it’s exactly what Smartbox offers. Do you want to inspire people to experience life? Join the pioneer and European leader of experience gifts! **The Role:** We’re looking for a **people\-loving, high\-energy Employee Experience Coordinator** to be the **friendly face of our Dublin office** and a key contributor to how our employees experience work every day. This role is ideal for a **graduate** (or early\-career professional) with a genuine **interest in HR, people operations, or workplace culture** , who thrives on variety, enjoys being around people, and isn’t afraid to grab a microphone or speak up in a room full of colleagues. You’ll sit at the heart of the office — blending **employee engagement, workplace experience, facilities coordination, and operational support** — helping to create an environment where people feel welcomed, supported, and excited to come into work. While your primary focus will be **Dublin** , you’ll also support other teams globally as needed. **Key Responsibilities:** **WORKPLACE EXPERIENCE \& EMPLOYEE ENGAGEMENT** **(What employees see, feel, and take part in)** * Champion a welcoming, energetic workplace experience that reflects Smartbox’s culture and values. * Design and deliver in‑office experiences, events, celebrations, and engagement initiatives that bring people together. * Partner with the People/HR team to support onboarding and offboarding experiences (badge access, welcome moments, swag). * Support delivery of the global employee engagement survey, including platform administration and basic insights support. * Use employee feedback to help identify improvements to workplace experience. * Own internal communication channels such as newsletters, intranet updates, and digital signage. * Administer the employee reward platform (Ben), including wallet funding checks and basic reporting. * Manage swag stock, ordering, and distribution across offices. **OFFICE OPERATIONS \& FACILITIES COORDINATION** **(How the office runs day to day)** * Act as the primary point of contact for office\-related queries and requests. * Oversee the smooth day‑to‑day running of the Dublin office, ensuring it is safe, organised, and well maintained. * Coordinate vendors including cleaning, maintenance, security, and office supplies. * Manage office\-related budgets, utilities, and invoices. * Support space planning, desk moves, and coordination with building management. * Ensure compliance with health \& safety requirements and company policies. * Maintain workplace documentation, processes, and best practices. * Support hybrid and remote working arrangements to ensure consistency of experience. **CULTURE, WELLBEING \& BELONGING** **(How it feels to work here)** * Act as a culture ambassador, helping Smartbox values show up in everyday office life. * Partner with the Social Committee to plan and deliver office events throughout the year, providing coordination and budget guidance. * Support wellbeing initiatives such as mental health awareness, ergonomic support, and fitness or wellness activities. * Assist with DEIB initiatives in partnership with the People Team, helping promote inclusion and belonging. * Help create an environment where all employees feel welcomed, supported, and able to be themselves at work. **OPERATIONS \& TEAM BACKUP** **(Critical but clearly contained)** * Provide backup support to other Operations team members, covering during annual leave, sick leave, or peak periods. * Support stationery ordering and general office administration for the Dublin office. * Coordinate health \& safety training requirements (e.g. fire wardens, first aiders). * Support People or Operations initiatives in other European offices when required, while remaining primarily Dublin\-focused. **Skills \& Experience:** * A third\-level degree is welcome — **HR is an advantage but not essential** * Strong communication and interpersonal skills * Comfortable using tools like Teams, Office 365, Jira (or keen to learn) * High attention to detail with a hospitality mindset * Comfortable using AI tools to work smarter * English is essential; a second European language is a bonus * Any experience in hospitality, events, admin, or customer\-facing roles is a plus * Background in hospitality, events, or employee engagement. (bonus!) **Why join us?** Because this role has **real impact** . You’ll shape how people feel at work, help create memorable moments, and be trusted as the **go\-to person** for the Dublin office experience. You’ll gain hands\-on exposure to: * HR \& People operations * Employee engagement \& wellbeing * Office and facilities management * Cross\-European collaboration And we’ll support your growth every step of the way. **About You:** We’re looking for someone who not only has the right skills but also embodies our guiding principles: **The Owner** – You take accountability, act with integrity, and treat challenges as opportunities. **The Ambitious Maverick** – You bring bold ideas, challenge the status quo, and are unafraid to take smart risks. **The Radical Optimist** – You maintain a solutions\-focused mindset, inspiring others with your positivity and resilience. **The Connector** – You build authentic relationships, foster collaboration, and create space for diverse voices to thrive. This role is much more about **attitude and potential** than years of experience. You might be a graduate, or in your first couple of roles — if you love people, we want to hear from you. You’re someone who is naturally **bubbly, approachable, and positive.** **What We Offer:** At Smartbox, we balance fairness, choice, and growth — supporting you in both work and life. * 🌍 **Transparency** : clear, fair salary bands and career levels * 📚 **Growth Mindset** : 4 Growth Days, Growth Leave \+ training budget * 🎁 **Flexible benefits** : personalize with health, wellbeing \& learning options * 🌈 **D\&I commitment** : Paid leave for parents, D\&I Day, gender equity in rewards * 🎉 **Unbox Your World** : YOLO day \+ paid sabbatical after 5 years * ✅ **Essentials** : health cover, pension, transport allowance, extra leave

title: Energy Analyst
description: **Wind Analysis Specialist (Renewables)** Location: **Newbridge, Co. Kildare, Ireland** (Hybrid working model with blended **working from home for three days** and **two days in the office** ) Duration: **Permanent** Reporting to: **Engineering Manager** Our client is an Irish Semi‑State Organisation, headquartered in Newbridge, Co. Kildare, has evolved into a modern **Climate‑Action Enterprise** . It now focuses on **Renewable Energy Development** , rehabilitation, waste and resource recovery, and **Large‑Scale Environmental Restoration projects.** Its mission centres on supporting Ireland’s transition to a Climate‑Neutral Economy by 2050 through **Clean‑Energy Solutions** , Biodiversity Regeneration, and Sustainable Land Management. The role will also involve completing **Wind Farm Layout Designs** , **GIS Mapping, Energy Yield Assessments,** and **Project Optimisation** to overcome constraints identified for projects in the planning phase of a development. It will ensure the successful delivery of key projects in the **Renewable** space. Success in this role will be measured by quality **Wind Layout Designs,** **Quality Resource Assessments** , timely delivery of projects, cost savings, improved team performance and contribution to the overall success of the department and organisation. The main duties and responsibilities of the role will include the following: * **Windfarm Analysis:** * Produce **Windfarm Layouts** using **WindPro** or similar software like **Windfarmer** or **Openwind** * Working with the engineering team colleagues to optimise solutions to **Infrastructure Design.** * Assessing **GIS mapping** and constraints for the **Wind Farm, Solar Farm** , and other **Renewable Energy Projects.** * Contribute to various **Wind Farm Studies** including **Telecommunications,** **Noise, Shadow Flicker** and **Visual ImpactAssessments** to aid the development and operation of projects. * Produce **Solar Layouts** using **PVsyst** or similar software. * **Wind Appraisal Resource Assessment:** * Assist with the **Field Deployment** and **Monitoring** of remote sensing installations for resource assessment measurements for the project pipeline, including **Wind** ( **mast and LiDAR)** and **Solar Measurement.** * Checking, management and **Analysis** of recorded data as required across the **Project Portfolio** including initial **Project Energy Yield Estimates.** * Complete **Site Suitability Studies** to characterise sites and develop project layout options, including working with **external Consultants** to validate and modify as necessary. * **Analyse** and **Optimise Wind Resource** and **Energy Yield Predictions** for new, developing, and **Operational Wind Farms.** * **Site Condition Assessments** for **Pre\-Tender Information** and input for **Turbine Selection.** * Contribute to the procurement of external services related to **Turbine Supply Agreements, Wind Data Management, Resource Assessment, Site Suitability Assessments** and curtailment analysis(noise, shadow flicker)associated with company related projects. * title: Field Service - Early Career Engineers
description: **Who We Are** Applied Materials is a global leader in materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every new chip and advanced display in the world. We design, build and service cutting\-edge equipment that helps our customers manufacture display and semiconductor chips – the brains of devices we use every day. As the foundation of the global electronics industry, Applied enables the exciting technologies that literally connect our world – like AI and IoT. If you want to push the boundaries of materials science and engineering to create next generation technology, join us to deliver material innovation that changes the world. **What We Offer** Location: Leixlip,IRL You’ll benefit from a supportive work culture that encourages you to learn, develop, and grow your career as you take on challenges and drive innovative solutions for our customers. We empower our team to push the boundaries of what is possible—while learning every day in a supportive leading global company. Visit our Careers website to learn more. At Applied Materials, we care about the health and wellbeing of our employees. We’re committed to providing programs and support that encourage personal and professional growth and care for you at work, at home, or wherever you may go. Learn more about our benefits. As a Field Service Engineer \[Customer Engineer] at Applied Materials, you serve as the direct liaison with customers, collaborating closely to install, maintain, and upgrade equipment. You'll use digital analytics for troubleshooting and apply basic diagnostic techniques to assess and address technical issues. Your responsibilities include performing preventative and corrective maintenance on various systems such as electrical, vacuum, mechanical, plasma, hydraulic, and gas systems. Additionally, you'll coordinate and communicate directly with customers to ensure smooth operations and exceptional service. **Field Service Engineer\- Early Career/University Graduates** Kick\-start your engineering career with worldclass training, hands on technical work, and international experience at Applied Materials—the global leader in semiconductor technology. This role is ideal for upcoming 2026 graduates, recent graduates or early\-career engineers who value precision, discipline, and structured learning, and who are adaptable enough to succeed in very different cultural and operational environments. **What you will do as a New College Graduate:** Field Service Engineers (FSE) are working in a state\-of\-the\-art facility and at our customer site in Leixlip, Co. Kildare. where they are the face of Applied Materials and an integral part of a vibrant and diverse team. They like to troubleshoot hardware, solve complex problems, and make equipment work better. **Hands\-On Engineering Work** * Learn to install, maintain, and troubleshoot advanced semiconductor equipment with high\-complex machine components * Work closely with experienced engineers in a cleanroom, high precision environment based on procedures for (un\-)scheduled and predictive maintenance including continuous improvement programs (CIPs) * Use digital diagnostic tools to solve real technical problems by machine data based predictive maintenance approaches (including AI) * Support customer operations and ensure a high\-quality equipment performance with a zero\-defect culture and high consciousness for employee safety and product quality * Build deep technical skills through structured, hands\-on learning on highly specialised Applied Materials equipment applications **Global Training Experience** You’ll participate in an immersive training program at our state\-of\-the\-art facility in one of our global training centers (Asia or the US), where you’ll gain: * Strong foundations in semiconductor equipment * Step by step technical training * Experience working in a highly structured, process driven environment * International exposure and cross\-cultural teamwork All travel and accommodation are fully supported. **We are looking for you:** * You hold, or are on track to complete (in 2026\), a relevant bachelor’s or master’s degree * Basic mechanical aptitude of pneumatics, hydraulics, electronics, vacuum, or thermodynamics * Experience diagnosing and resolving basic technical challenges * Familiarity with hand tools and their appropriate usage, including digital multimeters * Ability to read and interpret electrical and mechanical schematics * Familiarity with hand tools and their appropriate usage, including digital multimeters * Effective written and verbal communication skills * Clear communication skills and the ability to follow structured procedures * Willingness to travel internationally and work in a cleanroom environment * Be flexible to work both day and night shifts **Your mindset is important:** * Team player with strong interpersonal skills and a building trust nature. * Exercise judgement based on data analysis of multiple sources of information like plant documentations, schematics, and hardware data. * Find solutions with different tools to get the machine running again. * Explain difficult or sensitive information and works to build consensus. **Physical Requirements:** * Various physical movements such as bending, squatting, twisting, kneeling, reaching, climbing stairs and ladders, and extended walking and standing * Ability to distinguish between colors * Ability to operate hand and power tools * Ability to work in confined spaces * Ability to work in a noise\-prone environment (with consideration for noise sensitivity) * Wear personal protective equipment such as coveralls, hoods, booties, safety glasses, gloves, respirators, chemical aprons, and face shields, particularly in clean room settings * Flexibility for rotational shifts, including nights, weekends, and holidays as required by customer needs **Additional Information** **Time Type:** Full time **Employee Type:** New College Grad **Travel:** Yes, 20% of the Time **Relocation Eligible:** No Applied Materials is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, citizenship, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, veteran or military status, or any other basis prohibited by law.

(E) * Previous semiconductor manufacturing experience. (P) * Experience and knowledge of the following technologies: * Electronic, electrical, and electro\-magnetic devices (E) * Pneumatic and mechanical systems (E) * Vacuum technology (E) * Analog and digital circuitry (E) * Digital voltmeters, oscilloscopes, data loggers and hand tools (E) * RF, microwave systems (E) * Gas delivery, monitoring devices and the application of various gases used in etching processes (E) * Robot/Wafer handling systems (E) * Understanding of electrical and mechanical blueprints and schematics. (E) * Good computer skills, with competency in common office applications such as Word, Excel, PowerPoint. (E) **SKILLS AND ABILITIES** * Good interpersonal skills with an ability to work effectively in a team\-based, multi\-cultural environment, interacting with customers, service department personnel and others. (E) * Strong, detailed, effective verbal and written communication skills with management, co\-workers, internal and external customers. (E) * Strong work ethic \- on time to work and training, strong preparation for and on time completion of assignments, effective teamwork, etc. (E) **WORK ENVIRONMENT/POSITION \& SAFETY RISKS \& HAZARDS** * Work in a semiconductor manufacturing cleanroom environment fully suited in clean\-room garments, PPE and self\-contained breathing apparatus (when required). * The cleanroom environment contains electrical, pneumatic, compressed gas, mechanical, radiation hazards. * Work compressed workweeks and overtime when required *Warning: This job requires working on equipment or in work areas where electromagnetic frequencies may be present from some of the equipment which could possibly interfere with certain medical implants or devices.* **SAFETY REQUIREMENTS** * 100% adherence to customer and HTA\-IR Safety policy including the wearing of PPE as directed by policy (including but not limited to safety glasses, safety footwear, gloves, breathing apparatus, etc.). (E) * Ability to participate in and complete on\-time, all job\-related Safety training and certification requirements. title: Founding Engineer
description: Gontrel is live in Dublin with 10,000 downloads and a team in place. Now we need a founding engineer to own what comes next. Gontrel is a food discovery app, live in Dublin, over 10,000 downloads and growing. The idea is simple. Imagine TikTok but for food. You open the app, you see great video content from restaurants and food creators near you, and you find somewhere to eat that you actually get excited about. We're a small team and we move fast. We ship twice a week. The person we bring in here will have a real say in how the product is built. That's genuinely what this role is. What you'll be working on: * The recommendation engine: Helping users find content they love, faster. * Video infrastructure: Upload, processing, playback. It needs to feel smooth and it needs to work every time. * Creator tools: Making it easy for restaurants and food creators to post content, grow an audience, and get rewarded for it. * Discovery: Search, location, the stuff that connects the right person to the right place at the right moment. Stack: Node.js, AWS, Swift (iOS). You'll touch a bit of everything. Who we're looking for: Ideally we are looking for someone who has shipped a consumer app before. Someone who knows what it feels like to build something, put it in front of real people, watch what happens, and go again. That whole cycle. If that's you, we want to hear from you straight away. If you haven't done consumer apps, that's okay too. We'd just want to see something else that shows us you're the kind of person who loves building and shipping things that actually get used. You'll really enjoy this if you love the early stages, like to ship things and go again, care about how things feel to use, and want to own something not just contribute to it. What's in it for you: * Equity. You'll own a piece of the company. * Compensation. Competitive, we'll talk numbers directly. * Location. In person, Dublin office. How to apply: * We keep it simple. There's a short technical test linked here (5 days to complete): https://docs.google.com/document/d/1X5Cgepb5\_qvLOGz\-7xSc7\-48Mkm5hqo4HG7hHDGVAAU/edit?usp\=sharing. * Have a look at it and send your completed submission to info@gontrel.com. Already built something in Swift? Skip the test and just send us the app instead.

description: Where those who love pizza can be their true self. All Tronc goes to you, shared between our waiting and back of house teams. You’ll get this at the end of each pay period, along with your normal pay and it’s one of the best perks about working in hospitality. You’ll be part of a great team dedicated to delivering top\-notch service. From greeting customers to serving up our iconic pizza, you’ll create enlivening experiences that exceed expectations. Be yourself, bring your personality, and let every day be an opportunity to express your unique flair. No experience required. What you’ll do: * Greet and seat customers * Serve food, drink and desserts * Work in a dynamic and energetic environment Who we are: Where those who love pizza can be their true self. We’ve been around since 1965, bringing pizza to the UK and have since grown to a multichannel, international brand. Our people are at the heart of our success and everything we do; we believe you should always be able to express yourself. Inclusivity and respect are at the heart of our business and are valued by our teams, customers, and suppliers. PizzaExpress and Milano is a place where everyone is welcome, and everyone belongs. That’s why we assess each application on the aptitude to do the job and nothing else.

title: Fund Performance Analyst/Associate
description: We are partnering with a leading global alternative investment firm to hire an **Analyst** for its Client and Fund Performance team. This team plays a critical role in delivering high quality fund performance reporting and data analytics to existing clients, while also supporting new business development efforts. This is a unique opportunity to join a high growth, strategic function, working closely with senior stakeholders across front office, product, and client solutions. **Key Responsibilities** * Deliver fund and strategy reporting support to existing clients. * Source, analyse, and validate performance data at asset, fund, and investor level. * Monitor investment and fund pipelines to ensure accurate reporting coverage. * Partner with Product and Investment teams to understand strategy nuances. * Prepare client presentations and marketing materials for fund launches. * Respond to investor queries through data analysis and bespoke reporting. * Collaborate cross\-functionally (Front, Middle Office, Risk, Compliance, Legal \& Finance) * Support automation initiatives to improve reporting efficiency and data quality * Produce ad\-hoc analysis and reports as required **Key Requirements:** * 3\+ years’ experience in consulting, fund performance, or a related analytical role. * Background in investment banking, corporate finance, transaction services, or capital markets also considered. * Excellent academics, CFA exams highly desirable. * Strong understanding of private markets and investment dynamics preferred. * Advanced Excel and PowerPoint skills. * Strong analytical and data interpretation capabilities. * Excellent attention to detail and ability to deliver high\-quality work under pressure. * Proven ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment. * Strong communication skills with the ability to present data\-driven insights clearly. * Proactive, intellectually curious, and solutions oriented. * Collaborative team player with strong stakeholder management skills.

title: Geotechnical Engineer/Engineering Geologist
description: **Overview** AtkinsRealis have an opportunity for an experienced Junior Geotechnical Engineer with 3 or more years' relevant experience to join our geotechnical department in delivering complex geotechnical design solutions as part of our overall multidisciplinary team on infrastructure projects such as rail, highways, structures \& water. As one of Ireland's leading geotechnical consultancies, Atkins offers a comprehensive service in ground technology. Atkins' geotechnical reputation is built on the skills, excellence, and commitment of our people in all aspects of geotechnical engineering. You will work directly with all members of the geotechnical team across Ireland and shall be based in either the Dublin, Dundalk, Cork or Galway office, with the option to work remotely within Ireland in a hybrid work environment dependant on internet speed and connectivity. Your role * Preparation of ground investigation contracts documents for major infrastructure and commercial development projects. * Planning and procurement of ground investigation contracts. * Site supervision of ground investigation works for various infrastructure and commercial development projects. * Preparation of Desk Studies. * Designing earthworks, slope stability, shallow and deep foundations and retaining walls to Eurocode 7 under the supervision of a senior geotechnical engineer. * Preparation of geotechnical reports, including production of earthworks specifications, design drawings and designer's'and geotechnical risk registers. **About You** * Relevant years' experience of Geotechnical design, including major road, rail and civil projects. * Experience working in Irish/UK glacial till soils desirable, but not essential * A Level 8 Honours Degree/Bachelor's degree in Civil / Geotechnical Engineering or related field. * Proven experience of working in a fast\-moving engineering consultancy environment. * Site supervision experience in ground investigation works, foundation inspections. * Experience in desk study for geotechnical design using QGIS and available online resources. * Geotechnical design experience preferably experienced in design to Eurocode 7 in genral numerical software such as GeoStudio, Openground CAD. Etc. * Excellent interpersonal skills and the ability to lead geotechnical site supervision. * Excellent communication skills. * Excellent report writing skills with the ability to self\-check and produce accurate work. * A willingness to travel on short assignments both within Ireland and overseas. * Fluent in spoken and written English. * A valid full clean driver's license. Rewards \& benefits Explore the rewards and benefits that help you thrive – at every stage of your life and your career. Enjoy competitive salaries and a brilliant range of health, wellbeing, financial and lifestyle benefits. Make the most of a myriad of opportunities for training and professional development to grow your skills and expertise. And combine our hybrid working culture, international remote working opportunities, and flexible holiday allowances to balance a great job and fulfilling personal life. Be rewarded. Find out more. **About AtkinsRéalis** We're AtkinsRéalis, a world\-class engineering services and nuclear organization. We connect people, data and technology to transform the world's'infrastructure and energy systems. Together, with our industry partners and clients, and our global team of consultants, designers, engineers and project managers, we can change the world. We're committed to leading our clients across our various end markets to engineer a better future for our planet and its people. Find out more. Additional information We are committed to creating a culture where everyone feels that they belong \- a place where we can all be ourselves, thrive and develop to be the best we can be. So, we offer a range of family friendly, inclusive employment policies, flexible working arrangements and employee resource groups to support all employees. As an Equal Opportunities Employer, we value applications from all backgrounds, cultures and ability.

title: Graduate Electrical Engineer
description: **Graduate Electrical Engineer** Designer Group is seeking to recruit Electrical Engineering Graduates to join its growing engineering department. You will join a team of ambitions, innovative engineers challenged with delivering projects from our diverse range of clients from a commercial office fit out, to a prefabricated modular MV switch room, to a Biomass Boiler Plant in Africa. **About Our Company** Designer Group is a leading international mechanical \& electrical provider specialising in turnkey packages for the design, supply, and installation of M\&E services. Headquartered in Dublin, we employ in excess of 1000 experienced and dedicated colleagues across the Group. As an industry leader, we work with some of the world's largest multinational companies and our common purpose is delivering ambitious, innovative and sustainable engineering solutions, building client partnerships that bear testament to the standards we uphold. Our people are the foundation of our continued growth across all client sectors. We invest in our people and have a philosophy of continuous professional development. This investment has produced an innovative and passionate team that deliver projects for our clients safely, efficiently and to the highest standards in the industry. Our approach to everything we do is underpinned by our **Values of:** **Safe** **Trust** **Respect** **Challenge** **Deliver** This is an exciting time for Designer Group, with the fruits of our Engineering Our Future Strategy 2025 reflected in our substantial pipeline of work in Ireland, Europe, Africa and South America. The Engineering Team is an essential department within Designer Group and forms part of the foundation of every project. The Engineering Team is directly responsible for a number of key components in a project, delivering the highest standard of safety and quality. Designer Group is always at the forefront of the latest design and construction technology. From utilising design software, 3D modelling, point cloud scanning and 3D printing, Designer Group ensures their Engineering Team are equipped with the latest and best technology to ensure innovative solutions. **Overview of duties** * Undertake a full design review of drawings, specifications and schedules to ensure compliance with the applicable standards and industry guides. * Responsible for developing engineering alternative solutions, including detailed calculations and sketches for client approval. * Engineering software tools including Dialuxor Relux, Amtech Pro Design and Navisworks. * Review of the co\-ordinated construction drawings and documentation. * Prepare RFI’s and issue to the client. * Detailed review of specialist supplier packages for compliance with project specification and applicable industry standards. * Prepare and monitor procurement schedule in conjunction with the procurement team. * Prepare and monitor technical submittals for approval by the client. * Review BCAR preliminary inspection plan and coordinate with the quality team. * Monitor LEED and BREEAM requirements and submittals as required. * Preparation of operation and maintenance manuals. * Assistance with client demonstrations. * Review and provide attendance during commissioning stage. **Key Requirements** * A Level 7 Degree in Electrical Engineering/Electrical Services Engineering or Building Services Engineering. * Capable of working as part of a team and on own initiative. * Good knowledge of the current electrical, fire alarm and emergency lighting standards within Ireland. * Knowledge of key engineering software packages such as Dialuxor Relux, Amtech and Navisworks is desirable but not essential. **Benefits** * Health Care * Pension * Career Progression within the wider group * Accredited Engineers Ireland Employer for CPD * Pathway to Chartership with the SCSI * International Travel Opportunities * WrkIt lifestyle savings, learning and wellbeing platform membership * Overseas project placement opportunities after completion of the programme **How can you develop your career in DG** Our people are the foundation for our continued growth and so encouraging continuous professional development and pathway to Professional Chartership is a top priority. This investment has produced an innovative and passionate team that deliver projects for our clients safely, efficiently and to the highest standards in the industry. Our Head of Engineering began his career with Designer Group as a Graduate Electrical Engineer. His drive and ambition have allowed him to develop within Designer Group and we encourage all our staff to do the same. **Application Process** If you are excited about this opportunity and meet the qualifications mentioned above, we would love to hear from you. Our recruitment team will review applications and reach out to qualified candidates for further consideration. *Designer Group is an equal opportunities employer and positively encourages applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of sex, race, disability, age, sexual orientation, gender reassignment, religion or belief, marital status, or pregnancy and maternity.*

title: Graduate Environmental Consultant
description: **Overview** **Shape a sustainable tomorrow.** Do you want to join a team that asks questions and dares to see things differently? We are AtkinsRéalis, a world\-class engineering services and nuclear organization. When you join us, you'll help create world\-class sustainable solutions that connect people, data, and technology. We're transforming infrastructure and energy systems, growing partnerships with our clients, and bringing the most complex projects across the globe to life. Join us and engineer a better future for our planet and its people. Our global team of 36,000\+ employees speak over 70 languages and represents 130 nationalities across six continents. Our work covers a variety of sectors, including transportation, infrastructure, environment, water, energy, defence, technology and aerospace. On our Graduate Development programme, you take the driver's seat in your career. Grow personally and professionally, guided by inspiring managers. Say yes to opportunities that spark your interest, knowing you'll have training and the support you need to maintain a healthy work\-life balance. Reaching your full potential means being able to bring your whole self to work and feeling like you belong. Find out more about our global programme Different makes a difference. **Where could you work** As a Graduate environmental engineer, you'll enjoy variety, working on large and small projects with the opportunity to explore different career pathways, including consulting and design. You'll be a part of our vibrant and expanding multidisciplinary team. We're the gateway to planning, urban design and Environmental Impact Assessment within the organisation, comprising a vibrant network of people providing world\-class consultancy and advisory services. We're committed to providing the best outcomes for our clients, always with sustainability in mind. We share our understanding of the unprecedented challenges they face – climate change, rapid urbanisation, biodiversity decline, consumerisation, economic uncertainty, security threats and technology\-driven change – to create a better future. The work we do is important and influential, it shapes the way our clients think, enables them to make better informed choices and deliver their projects effectively in a world of disruption and change. As a graduate, you'll have plenty of opportunities to be involved in a variety of projects, ranging from energy and renewables, water, nuclear, property, rail, highways, international projects, planning and stakeholder engagement. Your role * Supporting with the coordination of Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs) and other non\-statutory environmental appraisals. * Supporting with coordinating a multi\-disciplinary team of environmental specialists inputting to environmental assessments and environmental design inputs. * Collecting and analysing data including conducting field surveys where required. * Carrying out desk\-based baseline constraints studies and appraisals. * Writing factual reports and sharing findings with multi\-disciplinary colleagues, clients, sub\-contractors and/or regulators or other stakeholders. * Supporting Senior environmental engineers / consultants, as required, in the delivery of robust planning documentation, to required best practice standards, in accordance with an agreed scope of work, and to the agreed delivery programme. * Working with colleagues, clients, environmental specialists and engineering teams to help promote sustainable design solutions and environmental enhancement. **About You** * Minimum Level 8 degree in Earth Science, Environmental Assessment \& Management, Environmental Geoscience, Environmental Impact Assessment, Environmental Science, Environmental Studies, Geography or Sustainability. * A passion for working in a consultancy environment. * Championing a culture where everyone belongs. * A desire to work collaboratively with a curious approach, challenging yourself to find new solutions. * Enthusiasm and drive to develop your skillset and seek out development opportunities within a supportive team. * Motivation to achieve professional accreditation as your career progresses (where applicable). * Comfortably embrace agile ways of working and learning new technologies. * Commitment to spend a minimum of three days a week in the office or on client site (project dependent). Being together in person helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more effectively, and accelerate your learning and development. **Training and development** Our graduate development programme is designed to give you an insight into many areas of work, and the chance to explore where your own skills and interests lie. It features a series of training events and modules, professional development, mentoring and on\-the\-job experience as well as practical skills development. From day one, you'll work on real projects that offer scale and variety, collaborating with colleagues and creating meaningful impact on the world around us. You'll work towards your professional accreditation (where applicable) \- whichever path you take, your future starts here. We're proud to have been an Engineers Ireland CPD Accredited employer since 2021\. This means you'll be supported by a true learning and development organization dedicated to your future. We can advise on the next deadline for applications (previous deadline was Sunday, 4th January 2026\). As our graduate development programmes are incredibly popular we recommend that you apply early as some roles may close sooner. Rewards \& benefits Explore the rewards and benefits that help you thrive – at every stage of your life and your career. Enjoy competitive salaries and a brilliant range of health, wellbeing, financial and lifestyle benefits. Make the most of a myriad of opportunities for training and professional development to grow your skills and expertise. And combine our hybrid working culture, international remote working opportunities, and flexible holiday allowances to balance a great job and fulfilling personal life. Be rewarded. Find out more. **About AtkinsRéalis** We're AtkinsRéalis, a world\-class engineering services and nuclear organization. We connect people, data and technology to transform the world's'infrastructure and energy systems. Together, with our industry partners and clients, and our global team of consultants, designers, engineers and project managers, we can change the world. We're committed to leading our clients across our various end markets to engineer a better future for our planet and its people. Find out more. Additional information We are committed to creating a culture where everyone feels that they belong \- a place where we can all be ourselves, thrive and develop to be the best we can be. So, we offer a range of family friendly, inclusive employment policies, flexible working arrangements and employee resource groups to support all employees. As an Equal Opportunities Employer, we value applications from all backgrounds, cultures and ability. **Salary** : €38,000 **Location** : AtkinsRéalis House, 150 Airside Business Park, Swords, Co Dublin, K67K5W4 **Hours worked:** 37\.5 hours per week

title: Graduate Facade Engineer - 2026 Graduate Programme (5553)
description: The Opportunity A career at Arup offers you the chance to make a positive difference in the world. Independently owned and independently minded, Arup is the creative force at the heart of many of the world's most prominent projects in the built environment. When you join Arup, you immediately become part of our global community working on some of the world's most recognised projects. **As a Graduate Facade Engineer At Arup, You Will** * Work within the façade team to develop façade solutions that align with the Architects aesthetic requirements and the Design teams performance requirements (with respect to the following performance criteria: u\-value, solar overheating, acoustic, structural movements, fire performance, etc) * Carry out engineering design / calculation and documentation of façade system design * Coordinate with internal and external parties on all façade related issues * Research and input into the following aspects of façade systems: performance, material, quality, maintenance, progress and cost * Develop digital tools to enhance quality and efficiency of façade design and to calculate the carbon footprint of the facade solutions being considered * Review technical submittals for adherence to project specific contract requirements * Undertake site inspections Does this role inspire you? We're looking for graduates who are currently pursuing a degree in Architectural studies (Architecture or Architectural technologist), Structural, Mechanical Engineer. We also value: * Proficient in sketching * Proficient in software packages (Revit, AutoCAD, SketchUp, Engineering design packages, etc). Please submit a a copy of your CV, Cover letter and most recent transcripts when applying.

title: Graduate Mechanical Design Engineer
description: **Responsibilities:** * Designing bespoke automation equipment for use within medical device sector * Working with our senior design team to bring innovative concepts to life through CAD modelling, prototyping and detailed specification for manufacture. * Use solidworks to develop complex multi\-part CAD assemblies and output detailed specification drawings for our in\-house tooling department. * Accompanying the senior team with client presentations, project reviews and helping to maintain our high standard of customer service. * Managing projects from initial concept, detailed design, assembly, testing, through to installing at the customer site. * Manage, plan and execute projects on schedule and within budget. * Ensure machines meet CE marking standards. * Ensure documentation/drawing standards for equipment meet requirements. * Procurement of material and components, ability to work closely and communicate clearly with suppliers. **Requirements:** * The person should be qualified to an appropriate level of engineering. * Minimum of 3 years’ Experience of Solidworks. * Experience of precision toolmaking is preferable. * Ability to manage projects from start to finish, ensuring projects are completed to a high standard and on schedule. * Good communication skills combined with an imaginative and creative approach to problem solving. * Ability to work as part of a flexible, dynamic team, while taking responsibility for individual tasks. * Excellent computer skills and competent using MS office. * Understanding of GAMP 5 documentation standards. **Benefits:** * On\-site parking * Refer a friend scheme * Bike to work scheme * Loyalty holidays scheme * Relevant training for development. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Application question(s): * When are you able to start? * How many years of machine design experience do you have in automation industry? Experience: * SolidWorks: 3 years (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Graduate Quantity Surveyor - Dublin
description: **About Entegro** Entegro is at the forefront of the telecommunications sector, specialising in the design, deployment, and delivery of end\-to\-end digital and optical fibre networks for future generations. The company collaborates with leading network operators and service providers to empower communities and businesses with reliable connectivity solutions. Entegro prides itself on innovation, quality, and a client\-centred approach to delivering network infrastructure. **Role Overview** We are seeking a motivated Graduate Quantity Surveyor to join our Commercial team. In this role you will support the management of commercial contracts, client invoicing, pricing activities, and contractual obligations across telecommunications infrastructure projects. This position provides excellent exposure to commercial and contractual processes within a dynamic, fast\-growing engineering services business. **Key Responsibilities** Assist with preparation and administration of sub\-contract and supplier contracts throughout the project lifecycle. Support contract pricing, commercial evaluation, and analysis of tender returns. Assist in preparing interim valuations, progress claims, client invoices, and reconciliations to ensure accurate and timely billing. Maintain accurate contract and commercial documentation, including version control, records of correspondence and contractual deliverables. Support measurement and quantification activities, including site measurement records and cost analysis. Contribute to monthly forecasting, cost tracking, cost\-to\-complete assessments, and commercial reports. Assist with client contract reviews, change\-control processes, and resolution of commercial queries. Work collaboratively with project delivery teams to understand commercial implications of operational decisions. Help maintain internal commercial databases and systems to support reporting and performance tracking. Support the Commercial Manager with contract compliance, risk identification, and value optimisation. Participate in engagement with internal stakeholders (project teams, finance) to ensure contract requirements are understood and commercial objectives are met. **Qualifications \& Skills** A bachelor's degree in quantity surveying, Construction Economics, Commercial Management, or related discipline. Working towards relevant professional accreditation (e.g., RICS, CIOB, SCSI) is desirable. Strong numerical and analytical ability with attention to detail. Good understanding of basic contract principles and construction cost management. Proficiency with Microsoft Office suite, particularly Excel and Power BI. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Ability to work both independently and collaboratively in a multidisciplinary team. Enthusiasm to learn and develop commercial skills in a technical / engineering project environment **What You will Gain** Firsthand commercial experience in contract management, pricing, and client invoicing processes. Mentoring and professional development within a supportive and innovative commercial team. Career progression opportunities in a growing international infrastructure services business. Exposure to innovative telecommunications projects with major network operators and clients.

title: HR Officer
description: **Description** Ryanair are currently recruiting for a **HR Officer** to join Europe’s Largest Airline Group. This is an excellent opportunity to join as we look to grow to over 800 aircraft and 300m guests within the next 10 years. The life of a HR Officer at Ryanair can vary quite a bit! They are the face of our HR Team and will be on hand to support our 27,000\+ aviation professionals across our growing European network. The HR Officer deals with a broad range HR issues and administration, from legal cases to drafting contracts and all the stuff in between, while travelling frequently to your assigned bases to meet with employees and answer an queries they may have. At Ryanair, you will gain vast experience very early in your career as you are entrusted and expected to act like a HR Manager for the 1000\+ employees under your remit to make daily decisions, offer advice and resolve all employee relations matters that arise. This is a perfect opportunity for a recent graduate seeking a challenge and to join a fast paced and dynamic working environment. **Duties Include** * Managing the entire HR function for up to 1000\+ employees in your assigned bases/ areas. * Monitoring employee absence and turnover to identify trends and deal with same. * Liaising, supporting and advising management at all levels across the business on all HR related matters, ranging from general queries, grievances to disciplinary investigations and legal case preparation. * Maintain the HR databases to ensure all files and the HR system is up to date and accurate. * Weekly reporting to the HR Manager on all HR issues in your area * Contract, payroll \& reference etc. administration. * Good knowledge of business and HR model to answer daily employee queries and make informed decisions. * Excellent writing skills to be able to compose important disciplinary etc. letters. * Working on ad hoc projects as and when they arise. * Legal case preparation for local litigation across Europe and for WRC \& LC. * Travelling across Europe to meet with employees on general employment matters, attend interviews and employee negotiations. **Requirements** * Relevant HR or business degree is essential * Ability to work in a pressurised and fast paced environment * Strong organisational skills * Strong interpersonal skills * The ability to work on own initiative * Excellent verbal and written communication skills * The ability to work under tight deadlines effectively and efficiently * PC Skills (Word, Excel, Power point etc) **Benefits** * Competitive salary * Discounted and unlimited travel to over 250 destinations * Defined Contribution Pension Scheme * Death in Service Benefit – Up to 2 times of annual basic salary * 20 Days Annual Leave – Increasing to 22 days after 12 months and 25 days after 3 years of continuous service. * Option for up to 5 additional unpaid leave days per year * Cycle 2 Work Scheme * Unrivalled career progression Competencies

title: Health & Safety Graduate (Construction)
description: **Role:** To support the Health \& Safety team in ensuring compliance with Irish health and safety legislation and company procedures across active construction sites, while developing the knowledge and skills to become a qualified H\&S professional **Key Responsibilities** * Assist with site safety inspections and audits on active construction projects * Support preparation and review of Risk Assessments and Method Statements (RAMS) * Help ensure compliance with the Safety, Health and Welfare at Work Act 2005 and Construction Regulations 2013 * Participate in incident/accident investigations and prepare basic reports * Carry out site inductions for workers and subcontractors * Assist in delivering toolbox talks and safety briefings * Monitor site activities for unsafe practices, PPE compliance, and hazards * Support implementation of the Construction Stage Safety Plan * Maintain H\&S documentation, records, and audit trails * Work with site management and subcontractors to promote a positive safety culture * Attend coordination meetings with project managers, engineers, and foremen **Qualifications \& Experience** * Degree or Level 7/8 qualification in, Occupational Health \& Safety, Environmental Health \& Safety, Construction Management (with H\&S focus) * Strong understanding of Irish construction site operations * Knowledge of risk assessment principles * Good communication skills for site\-based work with contractors * Competence in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, reporting tools) * Willingness to learn, adapt, and develop within a fast\-paced construction environment Pay: €35,000\.00\-€40,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * On\-site parking Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person
