title: ICT Support Engineer - Level 3
description: PFH Technology are currently looking for an **ICT Support Engineer \- Level 3** to join our growing team. **Location:** Dublin 1, hybrid **Contract length**: 12\-months (with expected extensions) **Responsibilities:** The ideal candidate will have at least **10\-years’ experience** in the following: * Configure and manage Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) for identity governance, conditional access, and MFA. * Implement Privileged Identity Management (PIM) and role\-based access controls. * Deploy and manage Microsoft Intune for device compliance, application deployment, and endpoint configuration. * Configure \& manage Microsoft Defender XDR solutions (Defender for Endpoint, Defender for Office 365 etc). * Monitor and respond to security alerts using Microsoft Sentinel and other SIEM tools. * Apply best practices for securing Azure resources, including network security groups, firewalls, and encryption. * Implement Zero Trust principles across identity, devices, and applications. * Lead investigations for security incidents and provide remediation strategies. * Maintain detailed documentation for configurations, policies, and procedures. * Must be able to show a high degree of awareness of cybersecurity best practices and concepts including phishing awareness, Identity and Access Management, Multifactor Authentication, Zero Trust Security Model, Purview specially Information Protection * Must have demonstrated experience of threat evaluation and security incident response * Must have exposure to and be able to demonstrate experience using an ITIL aligned Service desk and associated principles such as Incident and Service request management, Problem Management, Change Management, continual Service improvement etc. * Must be able to demonstrate excellent support \& troubleshooting skills for Level 3 Incident Management aligned with ITIL best practices (Incident management, Service Request and Problem Management). **Duties:** * Be willing and capable to work well within a team environment * Be able to clearly communicate technical solutions in a user friendly and professional manner demonstrating a customer service mind\-set * Provide third line support for ICT Operations and other ICT Teams * Liaise with other ICT technical team specialists within the tenderer’s organisation to complete projects, (the response should clearly detail the areas where the tenderer has technical expertise). * Be able to follow Incident Management Process. * Create and update our Knowledge Management system, SOP’s, and Runbooks. * To contribute effectively to organisational and team processes and adhere to policies including but not limited to Data Protection and Information Security * Be responsible for ensuring the IT systems and applications are maintained to best practice. * Recognise system deficiencies and suggest effective solutions while complying with company policies and procedures. * Research current best practice technology solutions and translate them into our environment * To participate in Change Advisory Board (CAB) and Problem Management meetings and to share information with the support teams. * Be responsible for resolution of all tickets within agreed KPI timelines. * Carry out any other duties as required. **Education:** Must hold at least one of the following but all three would be advantageous: * Microsoft Certified: Azure Security Engineer Associate AZ\-500 * Microsoft Certified: Security Operations Analyst Associate SC\-200 * Microsoft Certified: Identity and Access Administrator Associate SC\-300 * Microsoft Certified: Endpoint Administrator Associate MD\-102 PFH Technology, owned by Ricoh – a Japanese based global ICT company, is a premier provider of end\-to\-end ICT solutions and a managed services portfolio scaling from SMEs to large Enterprise organisations. We have unrivalled vendor relationships. We can procure, design, deploy and support all your ICT needs. Our ISO certified Custodian™ Cloud Services and Custodian™ Managed Services provide the technology and expertise to mitigate risk and reduce your costs immediately. We have a nationwide network of over 750 dedicated professionals, ready to meet your ICT needs, with offices in Dublin, Cork and Galway. We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at PFH. We do not discriminate on the basis of age, disability, civil or family status, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, race, religion, or any other legally protected status. We are committed to ensuring that individuals with disabilities or access needs are provided with reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process, to perform essential job functions, and to access the full benefits of employment. Please contact us if you require accommodation at any stage. IND1

title: ITSM Process Owner - Incident & Problem Management
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way. **Technology at Primark** What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. Our technology team is actively shaping the next wave of advancements. Engaged with innovative initiatives, your expertise will propel our business into the future. Collaborating with a creative team of tech enthusiasts, you’ll contribute your unique skills to fuel our technological advancements. **What You’ll Do as** **ITSM Process Owner \- Incident \& Problem Management** In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Own the end\-to\-end governance, consistency, and continual improvement of one or more ITIL process domains across Primark’s technology estate, ensuring alignment with business needs, ITSM strategy, and compliance across internal teams and third‑party partners, reporting to the Service Assurance Lead. * Act as the accountable owner for assigned ITIL processes (e.g., Incident, Problem, Change Management), defining, documenting, and maintaining policies, standards, workflows, and procedures while ensuring consistent adoption, governance, and compliance against agreed KPIs and SLAs. * Serve as the subject matter expert and escalation point for process\-related issues, supporting integration with ITSM tools such as ServiceNow, identifying optimisation and automation opportunities, and collaborating with Service Desk, Service Owners, Transition Managers, Product Teams, and Engineering to ensure operational supportability. * Define, maintain, and analyse process metrics and KPIs, lead performance and compliance reviews, and deliver regular dashboards and reports to the Service Assurance Lead and stakeholders, highlighting trends, risks, insights, and improvement opportunities. * Identify, prioritise, and implement Continual Service Improvement initiatives to improve maturity, efficiency, and service quality; facilitate improvement workshops and reviews; track progress and outcomes, ensuring alignment with broader ITSM and organisational objectives. * Engage and collaborate with internal service teams, product and engineering functions, and third‑party providers to drive process adherence and shared accountability, promote awareness of process value and behaviours, and support training and change communication initiatives. **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * 4\+ years of experience in IT Service Management with proven responsibility for managing or owning ITIL processes, supported by a strong understanding of ITIL v4 (or v3\) in enterprise and multi‑supplier environments. * Demonstrated experience implementing, governing, and improving ITSM processes across internal teams and external providers, including managed service providers and SIAM or multi‑vendor operating models. * Proficiency in ITSM platforms such as ServiceNow and associated reporting tools, with the ability to monitor performance, compliance, and improvement opportunities. * Strong analytical and problem‑solving capability, using data‑driven insights to support decision‑making, process optimisation, and continual improvement initiatives. * Excellent communication, collaboration, and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence process adoption, compliance, and behavioural change across diverse teams. * Desirable experience including ITIL v4 Managing Professional or Intermediate certification, retail or distributed business environments, leading CSI or process maturity assessments, and familiarity with Agile or DevOps ways of working and their integration with ITSM processes. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. **REQ ID:** JR\-8231

ship. **Responsibilities** * Develop, test and implement infrastructure solutions and self\-service tools, leveraging AI\-assisted development as a default workflow * Develop and maintain scalable title: Installation Engineer
description: **Who We Are** Applied Materials is a global leader in materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every new chip and advanced display in the world. We design, build and service cutting\-edge equipment that helps our customers manufacture display and semiconductor chips – the brains of devices we use every day. As the foundation of the global electronics industry, Applied enables the exciting technologies that literally connect our world – like AI and IoT. If you want to push the boundaries of materials science and engineering to create next generation technology, join us to deliver material innovation that changes the world. **What We Offer** Location: Dublin,IRL You’ll benefit from a supportive work culture that encourages you to learn, develop, and grow your career as you take on challenges and drive innovative solutions for our customers. We empower our team to push the boundaries of what is possible—while learning every day in a supportive leading global company. Visit our Careers website to learn more. At Applied Materials, we care about the health and wellbeing of our employees. We’re committed to providing programs and support that encourage personal and professional growth and care for you at work, at home, or wherever you may go. Learn more about our benefits. As a Field Service Engineer \[Customer Engineer] at Applied Materials, you serve as the direct liaison with customers, collaborating closely to install, maintain, and upgrade equipment. You'll use digital analytics for troubleshooting and apply basic diagnostic techniques to assess and address technical issues. Your responsibilities include performing preventative and corrective maintenance on various systems such as electrical, vacuum, mechanical, plasma, hydraulic, and gas systems. Additionally, you'll coordinate and communicate directly with customers to ensure smooth operations and exceptional service. In order to support our long\-term development, we are looking for talents to join our teams in Europe (Dresden, Germany / Dublin, Ireland / Grenoble, France). Within our Installation department, we are looking for our **Installation Engineer to support European regions** . The iTeam (Installation team) stands for the Installation of semiconductors production equipment across the European Region (mainly in Germany, ireland, France and Italy). We roll in, assemble, connect, and test the new systems until we hand over to our service team and our customers for the manufacturing of wafers to kick off. Being part of the Install Task Force is more than a job, it is a way of life. As a team **we travel approx. 75 % of our working time** , and also get opportunities to support installations worldwide. This position demands technical expertise, customer focus, team involvement and flexibility to travel. **Full training on our equipment will be provided through mentoring.** **Key Responsibilities** * Perform independently installation tasks (mechanical assembly, electrical connections, robot calibrations), from the integration to the inspection of the tools including troubleshooting in case of issues. * Follow all checklists and procedures and ensure correct procedures and schematics are available prior to performing work. * Test equipment with assistance from senior engineers, existing documentation and diagnostic techniques. * Respond to factory issues by communicating with other engineers and participating in escalation conference calls. * Participate in continuous improvement projects to reduce installation cycle time and costs. * Provide accurate reporting of status, hours worked and non\-conformances. * Adhere to the security policy and quality standards of Applied Materials and its customers. **Skills and Experience required:** * Bachelor or Master degree with technical background (mechanical assembly, equipment installation, maintenance, mechatronics, electronics, electrical, mechanical, or similar...) * Position open to junior profiles. * Troubleshooting skills to solve mechanical and electrical issues during system installation. * Ability to communicate daily pass\-downs and equipment status reports. * Project management skills to control installation project within budget, timeline and installation quality requirements. * Ability to work autonomously. * Good English communication skills both written and verbal. * Good Knowledge of the MS Office (Excel, Powerpoint) * The position requires extensive travelling throughout Europe (75% of the time, depending on overall activity requirements) **Physical Requirements:** This position often requires extensive walking, standing for extended periods of time, working in tight spaces, bending, squatting, twisting, kneeling, reaching, climbing stairs and ladders, working at height, lifting up to 35 lbs., the ability to distinguish between colors and working with electricity, the ability to work in an environment with noise that may be difficult for some individuals with sensitivity to noise, and the use of hand and power tools. Additionally, because this position generally involves working in a clean room, it requires the use of appropriate Personal Protective Equipment such as coveralls, hoods, booties, safety glasses, gloves, respirators, chemical aprons, and face shields. **Additional Information** **Time Type:** Full time **Employee Type:** Assignee / Regular **Travel:** Yes, 75% of the Time **Relocation Eligible:** No Applied Materials is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, citizenship, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, veteran or military status, or any other basis prohibited by law.

title: Intermediate Full Stack Developer
description: **Company Name:** Enterprise Registry Solutions Limited We are**ERS****,**a leading international registry provider operating out of Dublin, Ireland. We design, build, and operate registry solutions that power public and private sector operations and drive efficient business decisions around the world. Our people are what drives our innovation, growth, and well\-being. We are looking for a **Intermediate Full Stack** **Developer**to help grow our business and contribute to our future. Brief description of the role. Reporting to the Support Team Lead, other highlights include: The Intermediate Developer has deep specialized knowledge and experience that allow them to manage complex code, collaborate and are knowledgeable in the business domain they support. Their competency may even expand to help with marketing and overall business development. Key Responsibilities: * Develop software code using object\-oriented programming languages and is proficient in one of more code bases. * Ability to write simple and/or routine code (code is clear and shorter) with more consideration of the business and customer impact. * Responsible for maintaining and reviewing code and has the demonstrated ability to troubleshoot, debug and perform maintenance tasks independently. * Required to work with internal teams including project management, business analysts, DevOps and quality assurance to ensure code and applications are being designed and delivered as per customer expectations and requirements. * Ability to solution issues, provide estimates on tasks and assist in compartmentalizing sections of work. * Responsible to maintain technical documentation to assist with maintenance and upgrades. * Responsible to analyze, investigate and solve technical problems associated with code and applications and recommend improvements while minimizing disruptions and impacts to customers and other team members. * Demonstrated experience with the management of at least one full software development life cycle and may be involved in planning and deployment. Position Requirements: * BSc or Master’s in Computer Science (or related field) * 5\+ years’ software development experience * Strong skills in Angular (frontend), C\#/.NET (backend), and SQL * Experience with DevOps (CI/CD, deployments, environment management) * Proficient in Application Insights/logging for monitoring and issue resolution * Familiar with MS SQL scripting, Git, CSS/.Less, and JavaScript * Proven ability to design solutions and lead development cycles end\-to\-end * Experience with Agile methodologies (Scrum, Kanban) * Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and communication skills * Ability to collaborate across teams * Calm, structured approach to incident management * Proactive in improving performance, stability, and maintainability * Experience refactoring legacy code and promoting best practices * Contributes to scalable technical design decisions **What****we****offer:** At ERS, we believe in progress with purpose and are proudly certified as a Great Place to Work. We’re committed to creating a supportive, inclusive, and engaging environment where people can thrive. Here’s what you can expect when you join our team: * We believe in progress with**purpose.** * Innovative technology * A record of sustainable growth, * Investment in diversifying lines of business * We support your **growth.** * Career development and internal growth opportunities * Education support to help advance your skills * Opportunities to contribute to meaningful, high\-impact work * We care about**people.** * A supportive, inclusive workplace built on trust and collaboration * Recognition programs that celebrate contributions and achievements * A hybrid work schedule that supports work–life balance **A little more about us:** ERS is located in Dublin, Ireland while our multinational parent company ISC (TSX:ISC) is headquartered in Regina, Saskatchewan, Canada. ISC is a leading provider of registry and information management services for public data and records. Throughout our history, we've excelled at delivering value to government, to industry and to our community. title: JUNIOR ADMINISTRATOR - 4 month paid Internship
description: **BlueBuild Energy Ltd** *We Retrofit Ireland's Homes* **JUNIOR ADMINISTRATOR** **Paid 4\-Month internship /full\-time hours position – Ballymount, Dublin 12** **Location:** Ballymount HQ – Unit 3 Block K, Ballymount Drive, Ballymount Industrial Estate, Dublin 12, D12 T218 **Reports to:** Tracey, Deputy Program Manager **Rate:** Up to €15\.00 per hour, depending on experience **Hours:** Full\-time – 39 hours per week, Monday to Friday (4\-month fixed\-term internship) **Start:** Summer 2026 (or agreed start date) **About BlueBuild Energy** BlueBuild Energy is one of Ireland's most active residential energy retrofit contractors. We deliver SEAI\-grant\-funded upgrades, private retrofit projects, and local authority housing retrofit works (including for Dublin City Council), helping hundreds of households every year cut their energy bills and their carbon footprint. We have recently been appointed to new energy retrofit frameworks, and our programme continues to grow. To keep pace, we're adding to our administration team at our Ballymount HQ. **The Role** This is a hands\-on support role at the heart of our office. You'll work directly with our project, operations and finance teams to keep paperwork, compliance and day\-to\-day logistics running smoothly. No two weeks are the same – you'll see how an energy retrofit business actually works from the inside. This role is designed as a structured full\-time summer internship, providing meaningful administrative experience and exposure to how a busy retrofit delivery business operates. **What You'll Be Doing:** **1\. Care Pack Administration** * Support and help manage the administration of our job care packs – the compliance and documentation packs that follow every retrofit from survey through to completion. * Prepare, track and close out care packs for SEAI grant\-funded jobs, private customers, subcontractors and Dublin City Council (DCC) projects. * Chase, collect and file certificates from our contractors and subcontractors. * Book SEAI ESB re\-clip appointments and keep the schedule up to date. * Keep the filing system (digital and physical) in good shape so any team member can find what they need in under a minute. **2\. PSD (Project Safety / Handover) Forms** * Assist with completing and chasing Project Safety and Documentation (PSD) forms across all job types – SEAI, private, subcontractor and local authority (DCC). Responsibilities will expand as training progresses. **3\. General Invoicing and Finance Admin** * · Assist with processing supplier invoices for materials received. * · Help collate and log credit\-card receipts from the team. * · Assist in preparing staff expense reimbursements for approval. * · Flag anomalies to the finance team and Deputy Programme Manager. **4\. Stationery and Office Supplies** * Order care pack folders so the project team never runs out. * Keep printer ink and office consumables in stock. * Run the monthly general stationery order. **5\. Vehicle Fleet Support** * Work alongside Jessica to keep our van fleet compliant and on the road: * Maintain daily / weekly van check logs and follow\-ups. * Track and diarise motor tax, CVRT and insurance renewals. * Log and help chase reported maintenance issues with our garage. **What you'll get out of it** * Real, varied administration experience in a growing Irish SME. * Exposure to the SEAI grant process, public\-sector procurement and local authority retrofit delivery – genuinely useful for any career in sustainability, construction, engineering or business. * A supportive team that will invest time in training you up. * A structured full\-time summer internship providing practical workplace experience. * Up to €15\.00 per hour, paid weekly. **What We're Looking For:** **Essential** * Strong written and spoken English. * Comfortable with Microsoft Office / Google Workspace (Word, Excel, Outlook). * Well\-organised and able to keep track of multiple small tasks without losing any. * Reliable, punctual, and able to commit to the agreed hours. **Nice to Have** * Currently studying business, administration, construction management, engineering, sustainability or a related discipline. * Any prior admin, reception or customer\-service experience. * An interest in climate, energy or construction is a plus – but not essential. **How to Apply** Upload a short CV and a two\-paragraph email telling us: (a) Your earliest available start date, and (b) Why this internship interests you, Shortlisted candidates will be invited to a 30\-minute interview at our Ballymount office. *BlueBuild Energy Ltd is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome applications from all backgrounds.* Pay: Up to €15\.00 per hour Benefits: * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Junior Content Designer
description: **About Us** Droga5 is a highly acclaimed, creatively\-led advertising agency known for producing high\-impact, culture\-shifting work for major global brands. The agency was recently recognised as Global Network of the Year by AdAge. **The Role** We are looking for a highly creative, agile, and culturally clued\-in **Junior Content Designer** to join our fast\-paced Creative Department. In this role, you will work across a variety of brands to conceptualize and craft "thumb\-stopping" social content. This position sits at the intersection of traditional digital design and modern social media creation. You will be expected to deliver high\-quality production at high speeds—jumping seamlessly between designing a static graphic, shooting a quick TikTok, and laying out a beautiful client pitch deck. If you live and breathe social media, understand how to design for the feed, and love turning internet trends into brand moments, this is the role for you. **Key Responsibilities** - Social\-First Content Creation: Design, film, and edit engaging content specifically optimized for Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, and emerging platforms. - Agile \& Reactive Execution: Keep your finger on the pulse of internet culture. Work closely with our creative and strategy teams to spot a trending audio or visual format in the morning and have a fully produced, high\-quality post live by the afternoon. - Visual Storytelling \& Multidisciplinary Design: Move fluidly between various mediums, including static layout design, lightweight motion graphics, and native phone\-shot video production. - Presentation \& Pitch Design: Support the wider team with day\-to\-day client work by translating complex ideas into clear, compelling, and beautifully designed presentation decks. - Brand Guardianship: Ensure all content adheres to the specific brand guidelines, tone of voice, and visual identities of our varied client roster. - Creative Collaboration: Participate in brainstorms, share inspiration from your own social feeds, and collaborate with copywriters, art directors, and account managers to bring campaigns to life. **Skills \& Qualifications** - Experience: 1–2\+ years of experience (including internships or strong freelance work) in digital design, social media content creation, or a similar creative role. - The Adobe Suite: Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite, specifically Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, and basic After Effects. - Native \& Mobile Editing: You are incredibly comfortable behind a smartphone camera. You know CapCut, TikTok, and Instagram in\-platform editing tools like the back of your hand. - Design Fundamentals: A solid grasp of typography, composition, and color theory, with the ability to apply these principles to both social posts and professional presentation formats (PowerPoint, Keynote, or Google Slides). - Trend\-Savvy: A deep, intuitive understanding of social media platforms, algorithms, current trends, and what makes a piece of content shareable. - AI Production: You have experience with AI design tools like ComfyAI, Weavy and Higgsfield and show the eagerness to develop your skill using AI. - Adaptability: Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced environment, manage multiple micro\-projects simultaneously, and meet tight deadlines without compromising on quality. **What’s in it for you:** At Accenture Ireland, in addition to a competitive base salary, you will also have an extensive benefits package which includes pension, private health insurance, discounted gym membership, emergency caring facilities, and 2 days’ leave per year for charitable work of your choice! Flexible working arrangements can be discussed depending on the position. **Does this appeal to you? If so, we wish to hear from you:** Please advise if you have any requirements, so we can investigate into arranging reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. You can contact us at accessIErecruitment@accenture.com Accenture is an equal opportunities employer and encourages applications from all sections of society and does not discriminate on grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation, or gender identity, or any other basis as protected by applicable law.

title: Junior Health and Safety Advisor
description: Ayrton Group are seeking a Junior Health and Safety Advisor for a residential construction project based in the Lucan, Dublin region. The Junior Health and Safety Advisor will be responsible for assisting in all matters of Health \& Safety in accordance with client policies and relevant statutory provisions. This is an initial 12\-month contract role with the potential for extension. **Key Responsibilities:** * Conducting regular, formal site audits and carrying out inspections as directed in the Safety Management System. * Ensuring that a record of statutory inspections is kept for all activities and plant equipment. * Advising and instructing on various safety\-related topics (noise levels, use of machinery etc.). * Assisting Site Management in drafting and communicating Method Statements. * Reviewing existing policies and measures and updating according to legislation. * Inspecting premises and the work of personnel to identify issues or non\-conformity (e.g. not using protective equipment). * Performing incident reporting and near misses and follow up actions, identifying root causes, and implementing changes to prevent reoccurrences. * Reviewing subcontractor Safety Statements and Safety Compliance. * Reviewing Safe Work Plans for the tasks on site. * Managing Health \& Safety paperwork on\-site. * Managing and recommending PPE for on\-site employees. * Delivering Health \& Safety induction programmes for new on\-site employees and contractors. * Delivering Toolbox Talks as required. **Key Requirements:** * Recognised qualification in Health and Safety is required. * Minimum of 1 \- 2 years of experience in an on\-site Safety position is preferred. * Construction experience required. * Safe Pass and Manual Handling required. * Ability to work on own initiative and be innovative. * Excellent interpersonal and organisational skills. * Ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities. * You must be legally permitted to live and work in Ireland.

title: Junior Software Engineer, Platform
description: **At Semperis, our mission is to be a Force for Good. Starting with being a great place to work.** We believe that when people feel valued, supported, and empowered, they do their best work. Best Workplace awardee. **About The Role** We are seeking an exceptional **Junior Engineer** to join our Platform Group, where you will architect and develop shared infrastructure components that power Semperis's enterprise security and identity products. and maintainability * Architect distributed systems using microservices and event\-driven patterns that support enterprise\-scale workloads * Own critical backend services from conception through and platform resilience * Contribute to architectural decisions and establish best practices for backend development across the organization * Analyze system performance and data patterns to identify optimization opportunities and inform future platform investments What You Bring **Required Qualifications** * 1\+ years of professional software engineering experience, with expertise in backend development * Working proficiency in C\# and .NET Core, including modern framework features and performance optimization * Production experience with Kubernetes and container orchestration in cloud environments (Azure/AWS/GCP preferred) * Experience developing shared libraries, frameworks, or platform components consumed by multiple teams * Demonstrated ability building enterprise SaaS applications serving high\-volume, multi\-tenant environments * Strong collaborative mindset with excellent communication skills and experience working across teams to drive consensus * Bachelor's domain * Hands\-on expertise with Kafka, RabbitMQ, or similar message brokers * Knowledge of ElasticSearch or other distributed search/analytics platforms * Open\-source contributions or experience maintaining shared component libraries * Experience with observability tools (Prometheus, Grafana, distributed tracing) * Background in API design and governance for platform services * Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and infrastructure\-as\-code practices **Why Join Semperis?** You’ll be part of a global team on the front lines of cybersecurity innovation. At Semperis, we celebrate curiosity, integrity, and people who take initiative. title: Kubernetes Engineer
description: **Overview** We are seeking a Kubernetes Engineer to join our global infrastructure team and help scale, automate, and secure our container orchestration environments across on\-premises and public cloud platforms. As a Kubernetes specialist, you’ll work closely with DevOps, SRE, and security teams to deliver reliable, self\-service, and production\-ready Kubernetes clusters that power mission\-critical applications. **Key Responsibilities** **Cluster Management** * Deploy, manage, and upgrade Kubernetes clusters using tools like kubeadm, EKS, AKS, GKE, or Rancher. * Implement robust RBAC, network policies, ingress controllers, and security frameworks in Kubernetes. **Automation \& IaC** * Automate cluster provisioning and application deployment pipelines using Terraform, Helm, and ArgoCD. * Build reusable modules for consistent infrastructure delivery across staging and production. **CI/CD Integration** * Integrate Kubernetes with modern CI/CD workflows for rapid, safe application delivery. * Support GitOps practices and continuous deployment automation. **Monitoring, Logging \& Troubleshooting** * Implement observability for Kubernetes using Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, and Fluentd/Fluent Bit. * Troubleshoot performance issues, failed pods, memory leaks, and cluster degradation events. **Cloud \& Hybrid Deployments** * Operate Kubernetes workloads across AWS, Azure, GCP, and hybrid/on\-prem environments. * Use tools like Velero, Kasten, or Stash for backup/restore strategies in Kubernetes. **Collaboration \& Support** * Partner with application developers, SREs, and security teams to implement best practices. * Serve as a technical advisor on cloud\-native architectures and containerization s **Experience/Qualification Required** * Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) – Highly preferred * Certified Kubernetes Application Developer (CKAD) * Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist (CKS) * AWS Certified DevOps Engineer – Professional * Google Professional Cloud DevOps Engineer * HashiCorp Certified: Terraform Associate * Linux Foundation Certified System Administrator (LFCS) **Nice to have:** * Experience with GitOps tools (ArgoCD, Flux). * Observability tools like Prometheus, Grafana, ELK/EFK stack. * Experience with service mesh technologies (Istio, Linkerd). * Open\-source contributions in Kubernetes\-related projects * Spectro cloud, Incus, Cloud 9 etc… **About InterSystems** InterSystems, a creative data technology provider, delivers a unified foundation for next\-generation applications for healthcare, finance, manufacturing, and supply chain customers in more than 80 countries. Our data platforms solve interoperability, speed, and scalability problems for large organizations around the globe to unlock the power of data and allow people to perceive data in imaginative ways. Established in 1978, InterSystems is committed to excellence through its 24×7 support for customers and partners around the world. Privately held and headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, InterSystems has 38 offices in 28 countries worldwide. For more information, please visit **InterSystems.com** .

You’ll provide legal advice to management on strategic initiatives, business operations, regulatory matters, and compliance in Ireland. Lead corporate legal activities and ensure timely, effective legal support across the business, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts. **Key Responsibilities** * Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts and legal documents. * Advise on contractual, statutory, and regulatory obligations, ensuring ongoing compliance in customer engagements. * Conduct legal due diligence and research to support business decisions. * Provide legal counsel across corporate, commercial, and risk management matters. * Identify and mitigate legal and operational risks. * Interpret statutes, regulations, and contracts, and advise on resolving legal and business issues. * Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver clear, practical legal guidance. * Manage and support legal projects and, where required, supervise junior legal staff. * Report regularly to the Head of Legal on key matters and developments. **Skills \& Experience** * Qualified lawyer (LLB/LLM/JD or equivalent) with a current practising certificate. * 5–10 years’ experience, including at least 5 years in outsourcing, IT contracts, or licensing. * Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills, with solid knowledge of relevant laws. * Effective communicator with the ability to work across all levels and cultures. * Strong organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills. * Collaborative, proactive, and able to operate independently with minimal support. * Experience managing or mentoring team members is an advantage. * Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required. description: **Electrical Maintenance Engineer (Shift\-Based \| 4\-Day Work Week)** **Be part of something being built, not just maintained.** This is a great opportunity to join high\-volume manufacturing operation entering an exciting phase of growth and investment. As part of this, a **new Electrical Maintenance Engineer role** has been created. At our company, we aspire to be the premier research\-intensive biopharmaceutical company. title: Market Operations
description: A challenging role within a fast\-growing operations environment where the candidate will have the opportunity to expand his/her knowledge of the different products offered within the Securities Business. The role will require the candidate to assume responsibility for day\-to\-day processing of the full Domestic Markets teams processes. This will involve acting as a first point of contact for any queries related to the teams’ processes. **Responsibilities** : * A junior member on the Domestic markets team * Demonstrate ability to manage internal and external client relationships, to ensure the department responsibilities and objectives are met. * Coordinate and where necessary assist with any clerical tasks/activities within area depending on staffing levels and volumes. * Complete daily checklist ensuring all tasks have been signed as completed. * Daily monitoring of tasks to ensure all deliverables are completed in accordance with their corresponding cut off times * Act as first point of contact within the team \- liaise with other teams and clients to resolve escalated problems * Complete weekly MIS reporting with justifications for volumes, aged items and fluctuations. * Ensure Department Audit Requirements and procedures are adhered to * Ensure the completion of all UAT for system enhancements * Promote innovation within the team by looking at ways to streamline and improve current processes by technical enhancement or strategic positioning of functions within the group **Qualifications** : * Excellent verbal and written communication skills and strong interpersonal skills * Excellent Client service skills * Excellent attention to detail * Strong organisational skills \& ability to work accurately under pressure meeting strict daily deadlines * Maintain high work rates irrespective of fluctuating volume * Ideally from an Operations background with a sound understanding of financial securities * Agency \& Trust /Corporate Trust Operations advantageous

title: Mechanical Estimator
description: **Senior Mechanical Estimator** My client is a leading engineering and construction specialist delivering complex, large\-scale projects across Europe, with particular expertise in mission\-critical environments such as data centres. My Client provide integrated mechanical and electrical solutions from design through commissioning. Due to continued growth, my client is seeking an experienced **Senior Mechanical Estimator** to join its Dublin\-based pre\-construction team. **Role Summary** The Senior Mechanical Estimator will be responsible for preparing accurate, competitive, and strategic cost estimates for mechanical packages on high\-value projects. This position plays a key role in securing new work and supporting project success from the tender stage through to contract award. **Key Responsibilities** * Lead the preparation of **detailed mechanical cost estimates** across a range of large\-scale projects * Analyse **drawings, specifications, and tender documentation** to define the scope and identify risks * Manage the **full tender process** , including subcontractor enquiries and bid submissions * Source and evaluate **supplier and subcontractor pricing** * Conduct **value engineering** and cost optimisation exercises * Support **bid reviews and commercial adjudications** with senior stakeholders * Collaborate with design, commercial, and delivery teams to ensure alignment * Mentor junior team members and contribute to the development of the estimating function **Candidate Profile** * Degree\-qualified in Mechanical Engineering, Building Services, or a related discipline * **7–10\+ years’ experience** in mechanical estimating within an M\&E or construction environment * Strong experience working on **large\-scale or mission\-critical projects** * Solid technical knowledge of mechanical systems, including HVAC and pipework * Commercially astute with strong analytical and negotiation skills * Proficient in estimating tools and Excel * Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities **Salary \& Benefits Package** * **Base Salary:** €90,000 – €110,000 * **Bonus:** Discretionary performance\-related bonus * **Annual Leave:** 25 days \+ public holidays * **Car Allowance:** €6,000 – €8,000 per annum * **Pension:** 7\.5% employer contribution * **Private Healthcare:** Included * **Life Assurance \& Income Protection** * **Professional Development:** Training and career progression opportunities * **Project Exposure:** Opportunity to work on major European projects within a high\-growth sector

title: Microsoft Technology Engineer
description: We are seeking a talented and proactive Microsoft Technical Engineer to join our team. This role will be responsible for supporting, maintaining, administering, and enhancing the organisation's Microsoft technology stack across SharePoint, Dynamics 365 CRM, and the Microsoft Power Platform. The role blends technical administration, solution development, and business engagement, enabling users and teams to work more effectively through secure, scalable, and well\-designed Microsoft solutions. A strong focus will be placed on low\-code / no\-code development, continuous improvement, and automation. Reporting to the IT Service Deliver Manager, you will contribute to the support \& development of SharePoint \& MS Dynamics customisations, while also supporting the rollout, governance, and optimisation of Microsoft 365 and Power Platform technologies. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys cloud engineering, thrives in a collaborative environment, and is passionate about modern Microsoft technologies. **Key Responsibilities** **Microsoft Cloud, SharePoint, Dynamics \& Power Platform** * Administer and maintain Microsoft 365, MS Dynamics, SharePoint Online, Power Platform, and Azurebased solutions, including configuration, user management, and system optimisation. * Lead or support the design, configuration, and rollout of SharePoint Online sites, intranet, departmental sites, and collaboration spaces. * Manage SharePoint information architecture, permissions, and governance frameworks. * Develop automated workflows using Power Automate and integrate them with existing systems. * Support the rollout and adoption of integrated technologies such as Copilot and Power BI. * Perform SharePoint maintenance activities including backups, audits, and optimisation. * Provide user support, training, and guidance on SharePoint and Power Platform tools. * Track usage, generate performance reports, and drive continuous improvement. * Regularly audit permissions to ensure access is appropriately governed, minimising the risk of oversharing and unintended access. * Stay current with emerging Microsoft technologies and best practices. **Collaboration \& Continuous Improvement** * Work closely with internal customers to clarify requirements and deliver effective solutions. * Collaborate with crossfunctional teams to drive automation and digital transformation. * Keep up to date with best practices in Microsoft development and cloud technologies. * Support other IT team members to meet departmental service and quality objectives. **Summary of Requirements** **Technical Skills** * Experience of implementing security best practices for managing access and governance in MS Technology Solutions (SharePoint/Teams, MS Dynamics, Power Platform). * Administration and configuration of SharePoint sites, libraries, permissions, and workflows. * Administration and configuration of Microsoft Dynamics 365 architecture, including entities, relationships, security roles, and environments. * Working knowledge of Power Automate for building automated workflows. * Familiarity with SharePoint Online administration tools and capabilities. * Integration of SharePoint, MS Dynamics, and Power Automate with other systems (advantageous). * Knowledge of entire Microsoft stack including Microsoft Purview a distinct advantage. **Experience** * 3\-5 years' experience administering and supporting Microsoft 365, including SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, and OneDrive. * 2\-4 years' hands\-on experience administering and configuring Microsoft Dynamics 365, including entities (Dataverse tables), security roles, forms, views, and workflows. * 2\-4 years' experience designing and delivering Power Platform solutions, including Power Apps, Power Automate, and Dataverse, in a low\-code / no\-code environment. * Demonstrated experience auditing, managing, and governing access and permissions across Microsoft platforms to ensure secure and controlled information sharing. * Proven experience working directly with business stakeholders to gather requirements, deliver solutions, and support adoption in a production environment. * Strong experience with Microsoft technologies including Azure, Intune, MS365\. **Additional Skills** * Strong communication and interpersonal skills, including the ability to train and support internal users. * Ability to analyse business needs and translate them into actionable technical solutions. * Strong problemsolving and analytical skills with the ability to troubleshoot and optimise performance. * Commitment to continuous learning and staying updated with Microsoft technologies. **Desirable Skills** * Microsoft specialist certifications relating to Power Platform, Dynamics 365, Microsoft 365, or Azure are highly desirable, with support provided for ongoing professional development. * Experience with Teams, OneDrive, or other Microsoft 365 tools. * Microsoft Security, Compliance, and Identity Fundamentals certification. * Microsoft Power Platform Developer Associate or Microsoft Power Platform Solution Architect Expert.

title: Mobile Mechanic
description: **Mobile Technician – Full Time** Dublin (Field\-Based) United Tyres Autoservice Job Type: Full\-time, Permanent **Purpose of the Role** Carry out mobile jobs safely and professionally, represent United Tyres Autoservice to a high standard in the field, and make good decisions on when work should be escalated to the workshop or recovery. **Why This Role Matters** Mobile work is often the **first real experience customers have with United Tyres Autoservice**. In this growth phase, the role is critical. Done well, it builds trust, protects vehicle uptime, and ensures the right work flows back into the workshop properly. **Key Responsibilities** * Attend mobile jobs **on time** and complete in\-scope work safely and efficiently * Carry out repairs neatly and to a **high standard** * Capture clear **proof of work**, including photos and notes where required * Maintain **van cleanliness**, stock awareness, and proper equipment care * Communicate clearly with the **Senior Mobile Technician and front desk** when jobs change, fail, or need escalation * Represent United Tyres Autoservice professionally with **customers, drivers, and fleet contacts** **What Success Looks Like** * Strong punctuality and **safe, consistent workmanship** * Clear and professional **job completion and handover discipline** * Smooth hand\-offs when jobs require **workshop support, recovery, or parts** * A well\-maintained van and **reliable field performance** **What We’re Looking For** * Confident carrying out **on\-site repairs** (brakes, discs, batteries, punctures, basic mechanical work) * Good judgment \- knows when a roadside fix should **not be pushed too far** * Strong work ethic, **reliability, and attendance** * Professional attitude with a **calm and customer\-focused approach** Apply via Indeed or contact us directly at: 0872183327 \- Adrian unitedtyreswalkinstown@gmail.com Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €45,000\.00\-€50,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Employee assistance program * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Sick pay Experience: * Motor Mechanic: 3 years (required) Language: * English (preferred) Licence/Certification: * B Driving Licence (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: On the road

title: Network Engineer - (Ireland Remote)
description: **About Us** IVC Evidensia is the UK and Europe’s leading veterinary care group, made up of over 900 practices and referral hospitals, united by a shared purpose of happier animals, happier owners. At IVC Evidensia, we believe careers should be built without limits. Our people are supported to make more impact, access more career opportunities, and play an active role in their local and professional communities. Through investment in learning, innovation, and wellbeing, we create environments where our teams can grow in confidence, capability, and fulfilment—at every stage of their career. As part of Central Functions, you will be part of the teams that support and enable our network of over 2,500 practices across 20 countries, driving operational excellence, innovation, and strategic impact across the business. Our Technology teams are responsible for the systems and infrastructure that make that work \- everything our healthcare professionals need to deliver world\-class care. From keeping on top of patient records, health plans, client communications, diary management and dispensing, our teams have a lot to think about and need smarter ways to provide the convenience and expertise that modern Pet owners have come to expect. As a Network Engineer at IVC Evidensia, you’ll play a pivotal role in keeping our clinics and business locations connected, secure, and running at peak performance. Your work will combine technical depth, proactive problem‑solving, and expert support. **Key responsibilities include:** Safeguard network reliability and performance across a diverse European estate of clinics and corporate sites. Provide **3rd‑line support** for networking infrastructure, acting as a trusted escalation point for complex issues. Serve as a subject matter expert, supporting and advising colleagues with your deep networking knowledge. Deploy, configure, and maintain a wide range of networking technologies, ensuring consistent standards and high availability. Proactively monitor network performance, capacity, and resilience — identifying and addressing risks before they become problems. Administer and optimise solutions from Fortigate, Dell, and other key vendors to support evolving business needs. Produce clear, user‑friendly documentation tailored to both technical and non‑technical audiences. Manage third‑party suppliers, ensuring timely delivery, quality services, and strong operational outcomes. **What You’ll Bring** We’re looking for someone who is both technically strong and naturally curious — a problem‑solver who thrives in a fast‑moving environment. You'll be covering all Ireland based Practises \& Clinics, therefore a travel to sites would be required on a regular basis. **Your Skills \& Experience** Demonstrable experience in **network design, support, and troubleshooting** within IT infrastructure roles. Strong analytical thinking with the ability to **diagnose and resolve complex network issues**. Hands‑on expertise with **Layer‑2 and Layer‑3 technologies**, including their design, implementation, and optimisation. A **valid driving licence** and willingness to travel across Ireland for site\-visits. Relevant industry certifications such as **CCNA, NSE4, or NSE5** (highly advantageous). **What’s in it for you?** At IVC Evidensia, we’re committed to supporting your development and wellbeing. When you join us, you’ll benefit from: A role with real business impact supporting teams across the organisation Clear career pathways with progression opportunities within Central Functions and beyond Ongoing learning and development supported by tailored programmes and resources A collaborative, values\-led culture focused on care, innovation, and continuous improvement Flexible working with a remote first working policy **Benefits** Healthcare Cash Plan Cycle to Work scheme Green Cars salary sacrifice scheme Voluntary benefits: choose from a range of benefits to suit you Discounted staff pet care Access to discounts/cashback with hundreds of participating retailers **Diversity, Equality, Inclusion and Belonging** At IVC Evidensia we are committed to Diversity, Equality, Inclusion and Belonging, we are keen to hear from candidates from all minority and diverse groups. As a Disability Confident Employer, we are keen to hear from candidates with disabilities and long\-term health conditions and would be happy to discuss any reasonable adjustments needed during the recruitment process. We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Onshore Renewable Civil Engineer
description: Vacancy Number: 28382 Category: Engineering \- Civil / Structural Job Type: Full\-time The Onshore Renewable Civil group is a key part of Generation Engineering Delivery (GED) within Engineering and Major Projects. We provide a full range civil engineering services from feasibility stage through construction and ultimately completion of renewable infrastructure projects for ESB. As a Senior Civil Engineer, you will provide technical engineering services in the delivery of project works packages and advising on appropriate engineering solutions for various aspects of projects. You will work across a diverse range of renewable projects, with a particular focus on delivering ESB’s utility scale solar farm programme. You will also support the mentoring and professional development of junior engineers, sharing good practice and lessons learned across the group. Depending on business needs, the role may involve a full\-time assignment to a project site or working as part of a dedicated project team. **Key Responsibilities** * Fully embrace the ESB safety culture across all activities by taking ownership and demonstrating visible commitment to safety. * Provision of strong civil engineering technical expertise and leadership for challenging technical projects * Managing engineering works package(s) relating to the delivery of civil engineering works, site investigations, underground cable infrastructure projects, etc. as required including scoping, procuring, technical assessment, contract award and construction and contract management. * Building and manage relationships with internal and external stakeholders and manage client relationships in partnership with team leaders and project managers ensuring delivery of the client service and engage junior staff in continuous service improvement. * Manage and co\-ordinate all relevant civil, structural and environmental engineering activities required to successfully deliver projects to approved scope, budget and programme. * Providing technical support to ESB in its preparation of various submissions in respect of consenting, auctions, licensing, leasing, and scoping of various engineering packages. * Assist with mentoring and professional development of junior staff within the group including sharing knowledge and lessons captured on projects. **Experience and Qualifications** **Essential** * Minimum 5 years' relevant experience * NFQ Level 8 Civil Engineering Degree or equivalent required * Advanced specialist expertise and technical skills in civil engineering * Strong commercial and project management skills * Proven success managing major civil engineering projects for internal and external stakeholders * Ability to meet deadlines and handle multiple tasks * Self\-motivated, able to manage and prioritise work independently * Full driver's license and access to a car **Desirable** * Experience in administering FIDIC Conditions of Contract * Chartered status in a recognised professional engineering institution **Location** This position will be based in Engineering and Major Projects Office based in The Sidings, Grand Canal Dock, Dublin or Eastgate, Little Island, Cork office but travel to project locations is a key requirement of the role. ESB is committed to smart working – combining digital tools with a culture of trust and empowerment to enable flexible ways of working. The successful candidate may work flexibly within their jurisdiction with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration on at least 2 days per week. All smart working arrangements are subject to manager approval and ongoing review, based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the individual. **Reporting To** Senior Team Leader within Generation Engineering Delivery **Why Work with Us?** * Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future * Career development through mentoring and training * Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities * Sports and Social Clubs * Networking opportunities * Credit Union * Generous Pension * Access to staff well\-being programmes * Generous parental leave entitlements * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture * Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion * Great team environment working to our Core Values: Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted **Salary** €59,000 \- €70,000 per annum. **Closing Date** 12th May 2026 **Notes:** Your application will be held in reserve for 6 months should you be suitable for the role. The successful candidate will be required to evidence their right to work in the \<\<\<\< Republic of Ireland / UK \>\>\>\> prior to commencement of employment. **Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Statement** ESB is committed to being an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all sections of society and ensure that no one is discriminated against on the grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnicity or nationality, disability, age, citizenship, marital status, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation or gender identity, or any other basis as protected by law. Applicants who anticipate requiring assistance or reasonable accommodations for any part of the application or interview process may contact, in confidence, recruitment@esb.ie. **About ESB** Join us in our mission to achieve a net\-zero electricity system by 2040\. ESB is Ireland’s leading energy utility, with activities spanning electricity generation, transmission and distribution, energy supply, energy services and international consultancy. Operating in Ireland, Northern Ireland and Great Britain, we invest over €1 billion each year to deliver a new energy future based on reliable, affordable zero\-carbon electricity.

title: Operations Associate (FTC) - Grafton Street
description: **WHY JOIN ALO?** Mindful movement. It’s at the core of why we do what we do at ALO—it’s our calling. Because mindful movement in the studio leads to better living. It changes who yogis are off the mat, making their lives and their communities better. That’s the real meaning of studio\-to\-street: taking the consciousness from practice on the mat and putting it into practice in life. **Objective** The Operations Associate is critical in the store team and is responsible for driving to profitability and efficiency. The Operations Associate will work closely with the Operations \& Visual Manager \& Operations Lead in initiating, completing work, and providing feedback to the team to achieve optimal results supporting total store operations. **This is a 6\-month, fixed\-term contract.** **Key Job Responsibilities** * Execute inventory management processes effectively with a focus on maximizing inventory accuracy and reducing shrink * Investigate and root cause inventory accuracy issues, partnering with the General Manager to escalate process or system gaps * Support execution of key operational functions including shipment, receiving and processing, reverse transfers, sales floor recovery, intra\-day sales floor replenishment, floor set updates, and supply management * Maintain and champion strong visual standards for the sales floor * Work closely with reporting, budgets, expenses, inventory control, supplies, vendor relationships, technology, and facilities to ensure maintained and maximized * Support a safe work environment and efficient operation through strong stockroom standards and processes * Move dynamically on the retail floor to assess and fulfill the needs of the business, team and guests * Execute company\-level operations\-focused directives, projects and initiates, in partnership with the Operations \& Visual Manager \& Operations Lead. * Assists in processing shipment and ensuring product flow; including but not limited to visual merchandising placement and daily replenishment excellence * Demonstrate strong business acumen by leveraging metrics to support business\-driving strategies * Resolve client needs quickly \& effectively ensuring customer satisfaction * Identify product concerns and communicate inventory needs to support the business goals * Support other areas of the business, including Sales \& Service and Cashier, as needed **Operations Associate Qualifications** * Preferred prior work experience in a client\-centric, sales \& operational environment * Passion for customer service and delivering exceptional experiences * High energy, upbeat and enthusiastic with the ability to integrate fun and work * Self\-motivated with a desire to achieve results and excel individually, and as a team * Fosters genuine connection through empathy, compassion, integrity and building trusting relationships * Aligns with and embodies ALO's Guiding Principles * Proof of right to live and work in the UK **Operations Associate Schedule** Part\-Time associates are expected to work up to 29 hours per week; Full\-Time associates are expected to work 32\-40 hours per week with 100% of their work week in operations and floor support. All employees' availability must suit the needs of the business, which are subject to change. The General Manager determines availability needs and creates a schedule including morning, evening, and weekend shifts, which may be subject to change. All Employees are expected to be able to work from the week of and including “Black Friday” until the end of the holiday season, first week of January. **Diversity and Inclusion statement** As an Equal Opportunity Employer, ALO does not discriminate against applicants or employees because of race, colour, creed, religion, sex, national origin, veteran status, disability, age, citizenship, marital or domestic/civil partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression or because of any other status or condition protected by statutory law. We are committed to an active Inclusion, Diversity and Equal Opportunities Policy, which starts with our recruitment and selection process, and we are happy to talk flexible working. **Interview Process adjustments** If you consider yourself to require reasonable adjustments to any part of our recruitment process, we invite you to share those requirements with us when completing your application or by emailing europehr@aloyoga.com. We will make every effort to ensure your needs are met to provide a fair and transparent process of assessment. For CA residents, Job Applicant Privacy Policy HERE.

title: Options Sales Middle Office Analyst
description: Joining the Client team as a Sales Operations Analyst supporting the Options Sales business, you will be responsible for Susquehanna’s options sales desk’s post trade processes to provide a seamless client experience. This role involves working closely with our sales traders, traders, and counterparties. Due to the high volume of trades, we are looking for candidates who have exceptional attention to detail. This role will offer a great exposure to a front office Sales \& Trading environment and will be an excellent opportunity if you have strong communication skills and an interest in financial markets. Being directly exposed to client interactions, a good level of commercial awareness is desired. **In this role you will:** * Responsible for post trade processes * Execution and allocation of orders through internal/external systems * Act as a first point of contact for any issues related to crossing and settling trades across multiple exchanges, countries and clients * Address each client’s specific post trade requirements and tailor the process to their needs. For example, clients trading on CFD or making sure trades with FTT are accounted for correctly. * Identify opportunities to improve the overall process, automate tools where possible in order to enhance the broader desk’s performance and efficiency. This could include working with the static data team to improve our flex trading process and working with app support and developers to improve the desks tools * Liaise with other teams to develop and work on ad hoc projects aimed at improving the team’s workflow **What we're looking for:** * Minimum of a Bachelors’ degree * Ability to prioritise and complete multiple tasks in a fast paced, dynamic environment * High level of proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel. Python a plus * Strong communicator (verbal and written) * Ability to work under pressure and multi\-task * Ambitious and self\-motivated, not afraid to ask questions and use own initiative * Effective problem solving and analytical skills; including effective troubleshooting in a fast\-paced, time\-critical environment * Experience in financial markets is not required although we welcome those with experience * Excellent attention to detail **About Susquehanna** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting\-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together.

title: Oracle Flexcube Business Analyst (Contractor – 6 Months)
description: MUFG Investor Services is a trusted partner to many of the world’s largest public and private funds, providing asset servicing and operational solutions built for alternatives. With over $1 trillion in client assets under administration, we offer fund administration, banking, payments, fund financing, foreign exchange overlay, corporate and regulatory services, custody, business consulting, and more. Operating from 17 locations worldwide, we help clients mitigate risk, enhance efficiency, and navigate the operational complexities of today’s investment management landscape. As a division of Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (MUFG), one of the world’s largest financial institutions with approximately $3 trillion in assets, we combine deep expertise with the strength and stability of a leading financial institution. To learn more, visit us at www.mufg\-investorservices.com. **Job Description** As an Oracle Flexcube Business Analyst, you will play a critical role in helping MUFG Investor Services get more value from its core banking platform. We run Oracle Flexcube Universal Banking as the foundation of our banking operations – covering payments, treasury, foreign exchange, money markets, nostro reconciliation, SWIFT connectivity and more. The platform has grown significantly in capability and there is a real opportunity to close the gap between what Flexcube can do and what we currently use. You will work across IT and the business to identify standard features we have not yet activated, assess bespoke components built in earlier phases to determine whether they can be replaced by native functionality, and drive resolution of open configuration and change requests. You will work closely with the Banking Family Lead in IT and collaborate across Application Management, Banking Operations, and Product Management. You will also be a key point of contact with Oracle Support, coordinating on open tickets, change requests, and configuration queries – bringing clarity and momentum to engagements that have historically required significant back\-and\-forth. This is a hands\-on, delivery\-focused role that combines platform knowledge with strong stakeholder management and the ability to produce clear, well\-evidenced analysis and documentation. **Key Responsibilities** **Platform Capability Assessment** * Conduct a structured review of licensed Flexcube modules to identify features and configurations available but not currently activated or used. * Benchmark current usage across payments, standing instructions, foreign exchange, money markets, nostro reconciliation, and reporting against the full standard product capability. * Produce a prioritised inventory of unutilised capabilities with assessed business value and implementation complexity. **Bespoke Component Rationalisation** * Review customisations and bespoke components built in prior versions to determine whether equivalent standard functionality now exists in the current release. * Prepare structured assessments for each bespoke item: functional equivalence, migration complexity, risk, and recommended action (retire, retain, or replace). * Work with the Banking Family Lead and Application Management to validate findings and agree a rationalisation roadmap. **Backlog Management and Resolution** * Triage, document, and drive resolution of open items in the Flexcube Jira backlog, spanning configuration issues, change requests, business queries, and scripting requirements. * Distinguish clearly between items requiring Oracle involvement, items resolvable through internal configuration, and items needing formal change requests. * Maintain backlog hygiene: correct categorisation, clear ownership, and defined next steps for all items. **Oracle Engagement and Coordination** * Act as the primary coordination point for Oracle Support on open tickets and change requests, working alongside the Banking Family Lead and Application Management. * Prepare clear, well\-evidenced submissions to reduce round\-trip delays and accelerate resolution. * Track Oracle commitments, patch schedules, and responses, escalating where timelines slip. **Business and IT Collaboration** * Work with Product Management to translate business requirements into Flexcube configuration or change specifications. * Engage Banking Operations, Application Management, and project teams to gather requirements, validate solutions, and support UAT. * Produce high\-quality documentation: business requirements, functional specifications, solution designs, and training notes as required. **Reporting and Governance** * Provide regular progress updates to the Banking Family Lead on backlog status, capability findings, and Oracle engagement. * Flag risks and issues promptly, particularly where unresolved items represent operational or compliance exposure. **Qualifications** **You Have:** **Flexcube \& Core Banking Knowledge** * 5–8\+ years of hands\-on experience working with Oracle Flexcube Universal Banking as a business analyst, functional consultant, or implementation specialist. * Deep understanding of core banking operations including payments (domestic and cross\-border), SWIFT messaging, foreign exchange, money markets, and account management. * Experience engaging with Oracle Financial Services on support tickets, change requests, and product configuration – ideally including post\-upgrade optimisation work. * Ability to assess bespoke platform customisations objectively and build evidence\-based cases for rationalisation or replacement. **Business Analysis** * Proven ability to analyse and document complex functional requirements and translate them into clear specifications suitable for configuration or development. * Experience managing and triaging product backlogs, working across IT and business stakeholders to prioritise effectively. * Strong written communication skills; able to produce high\-quality documentation independently without extensive supervision. * Excellent stakeholder management – comfortable bridging technical IT teams and non\-technical business users simultaneously. **Technical Skills** * SQL proficiency – able to write and interpret queries to interrogate Flexcube data, validate configurations, and support analysis work. * Familiarity with Flexcube integration patterns (web services, REST/SOAP APIs) and data flows between Flexcube and downstream systems. * Comfortable working with Jira or similar backlog management tools. **Additional Information** Take a look at our careers site and you’ll find everything you’d expect from a career with the fastest\-growing business at one of the world’s largest financial groups. Now take another look. Because it’s how we defy expectations that really defines us. You’ll feel that difference in all kinds of ways. Our vibrant CULTURE. Connected team. Love of innovation, laser client focus, and next\-level LEARNING \& DEVELOPMENT. Oh, and we really walk the talk when it comes to HYBRID WORKING. So, why settle for the ordinary? Apply now for a Brilliantly Different career. We thank all candidates for applying; however, only those proceeding to the interview stage will be contacted.
